questionFr: Le feu rouge d'un passage à niveau clignote mais les barrières sont levées. Quelles affirmations sont vraies ? (Sélectionnez toutes les bonnes réponses)
questionEn: The red light at a level crossing is flashing but the barriers are up. Which statements are true? (Select all correct answers)

A.fr: L'arrêt est obligatoire — le feu rouge clignotant est l'ordre d'arrêt absolu
A.en: Stopping is mandatory — the flashing red light is the absolute stop order

B.fr: La position des barrières est sans importance quand le feu rouge clignote
B.en: The barrier position is irrelevant when the red light is flashing

C.en: Slowing down and passing carefully is sufficient

D.fr: On peut passer rapidement si les barrières sont levées
D.en: You can pass quickly if the barriers are up

explanationFr: Le feu rouge clignotant à un passage à niveau signifie ARRÊT ABSOLU, quelle que soit la position des barrières. Les barrières peuvent être en panne, ou le train peut approcher à très grande vitesse. Franchir ce feu est une infraction de 4e classe (perte de 4 points).
explanationEn: The flashing red light at a level crossing means ABSOLUTE STOP, regardless of barrier position. The barriers could be malfunctioning, or a train may be approaching at high speed. Crossing this light is a 4th-class offence (loss of 4 points).
trapNote: Both A and B are correct — the stop is absolute AND barrier position does not matter. The light is the primary signal.

distractorNotes.C: Slowing down is not sufficient — a complete stop is mandatory.
distractorNotes.D: Barriers up does NOT mean it is safe — the light takes priority.

vocabulary.definition: A red light that flashes on and off, indicating an absolute stop requirement at level crossings

questionFr: Pourquoi est-il impossible d'évaluer correctement la distance d'un train en approche ?
questionEn: Why is it impossible to correctly judge the distance of an approaching train?

A.fr: Parce que les trains sont silencieux

B.fr: Parce que la vitesse élevée et la taille du train créent une illusion — il semble plus loin et plus lent qu'il ne l'est
B.en: Because the high speed and size of the train create an illusion — it appears farther and slower than it is

C.fr: Parce que les rails déforment la perception visuelle
C.en: Because the rails distort visual perception

D.fr: Parce qu'on ne voit jamais le train arriver
D.en: Because you never see the train coming

explanationFr: Un train se déplace à grande vitesse (un TER peut rouler à 160 km/h, un TGV à 320 km/h) et sa taille massive crée une illusion d'optique : il semble se déplacer plus lentement et être plus éloigné qu'il ne l'est en réalité. C'est pourquoi on ne doit JAMAIS essayer de « passer avant le train ».
explanationEn: A train moves at high speed (a regional train can travel at 160 km/h, a TGV at 320 km/h) and its massive size creates an optical illusion: it appears to move more slowly and be farther away than it actually is. This is why you must NEVER try to 'beat the train'.
trapNote: The optical illusion is the key concept — the train looks slow and far but arrives in seconds. This is heavily tested.

distractorNotes.A: Trains are not silent — they produce significant noise, but noise alone doesn't help judge distance.
distractorNotes.C: Rails don't distort perception — it's the train's speed and size.
distractorNotes.D: You can often see the train, but you misjudge its speed.

vocabulary.definition: Where a road crosses a railway track at the same level
A visual effect that deceives the brain about size, speed, or distance

questionFr: En tunnel, quelle fréquence radio devez-vous écouter ?
questionEn: In a tunnel, which radio frequency should you tune to?

A.fr: La fréquence indiquée à l'entrée du tunnel (généralement 107.7 MHz)
A.en: The frequency indicated at the tunnel entrance (usually 107.7 MHz)

B.fr: N'importe quelle station FM

C.fr: La radio n'est pas nécessaire en tunnel
C.en: Radio is not necessary in a tunnel

D.fr: La fréquence de la police nationale

explanationFr: À l'entrée des tunnels équipés, un panneau indique la fréquence radio (souvent 107.7 MHz) à écouter pour recevoir les consignes de sécurité en cas d'incident. Cette fréquence permet à l'exploitant du tunnel de diffuser des messages d'urgence.
explanationEn: At the entrance of equipped tunnels, a sign indicates the radio frequency (often 107.7 MHz) to tune into for safety instructions in case of an incident. This frequency allows the tunnel operator to broadcast emergency messages.
trapNote: 107.7 MHz is the standard autoroute info frequency in France — and it is also used in tunnels for emergency broadcasts.

distractorNotes.B: Only the specific tunnel frequency will carry emergency messages.
distractorNotes.C: Radio is an essential safety tool in tunnels.
distractorNotes.D: The police frequency is not broadcast to the public.

vocabulary.definition: The specific FM frequency designated for safety information in a tunnel

questionFr: Quelle est la distance minimale de sécurité recommandée en tunnel ?
questionEn: What is the recommended minimum following distance in a tunnel?

A.fr: 50 mètres ou 2 secondes

B.fr: 100 mètres ou le double de la distance normale
B.en: 100 metres or double the normal distance

explanationFr: En tunnel, la distance de sécurité recommandée est de 100 mètres minimum, soit environ le double de la distance normale. Des repères de distance sont souvent peints sur les murs du tunnel pour aider les conducteurs.
explanationEn: In a tunnel, the recommended following distance is at least 100 metres, approximately double the normal distance. Distance markers are often painted on the tunnel walls to help drivers.
trapNote: Double the normal distance — 100m, not 50m. The confined space makes accidents much more dangerous.

distractorNotes.A: 50 metres is roughly the normal following distance — tunnels need double.
distractorNotes.C: 20 metres is dangerously close in any driving situation.
distractorNotes.D: Tunnels require increased distance due to confined conditions.

vocabulary.definition: Painted or posted indicators on tunnel walls showing the distance between vehicles

questionFr: Quel est le rôle du système de ventilation dans un tunnel routier ?
questionEn: What is the role of the ventilation system in a road tunnel?

A.fr: Uniquement rafraîchir l'air pour le confort
A.en: Only to cool the air for comfort

B.fr: Évacuer les gaz d'échappement en circulation normale et contrôler les fumées en cas d'incendie
B.en: Evacuate exhaust gases during normal traffic and control smoke in case of fire

C.fr: Créer un courant d'air pour pousser les véhicules
C.en: Create an air current to push vehicles

D.fr: Empêcher la pluie d'entrer dans le tunnel
D.en: Prevent rain from entering the tunnel

explanationFr: Le système de ventilation en tunnel a une double fonction : en conditions normales, il évacue les polluants (CO, NOx, particules). En cas d'incendie, il contrôle la direction des fumées pour maintenir un air respirable côté évacuation.
explanationEn: The tunnel ventilation system has a dual function: in normal conditions, it evacuates pollutants (CO, NOx, particles). In case of fire, it controls smoke direction to maintain breathable air on the evacuation side.
trapNote: The fire safety role is the critical one — ventilation directs smoke AWAY from the evacuation route.

distractorNotes.A: Comfort is a minor benefit — the primary roles are pollution control and fire safety.
distractorNotes.C: Ventilation does not move vehicles.
distractorNotes.D: Rain protection is handled by the tunnel structure, not ventilation.

vocabulary.definition: The mechanical system that circulates and exchanges air within a tunnel
Pollutant gases emitted by vehicle engines

questionFr: Pourquoi les postes d'appel d'urgence (PAU) sont-ils préférables au téléphone portable en tunnel ? (Sélectionnez toutes les bonnes réponses)
questionEn: Why are emergency call points (PAU) preferable to mobile phones in a tunnel? (Select all correct answers)

A.fr: Ils contactent directement le PC sécurité du tunnel
A.en: They connect directly to the tunnel safety control room

B.fr: Ils localisent automatiquement l'appelant

C.fr: Ils fonctionnent même sans réseau mobile
C.en: They work even without mobile network

D.fr: Ils permettent de recharger son téléphone
D.en: They allow you to charge your phone

explanationFr: Les PAU sont préférables car : 1) Ils contactent directement le PC sécurité du tunnel (pas le 112 généraliste). 2) Ils localisent automatiquement l'appelant dans le tunnel. 3) Ils fonctionnent indépendamment du réseau mobile, qui peut être absent en tunnel. Ils sont situés tous les 200 m environ.
explanationEn: PAU are preferable because: 1) They connect directly to the tunnel safety control room (not the general 112). 2) They automatically locate the caller in the tunnel. 3) They work independently of mobile networks, which may be unavailable in tunnels. They are located every 200 m approximately.
trapNote: Three advantages of PAU over phones: direct line, auto-location, network-independent. All three make them the superior choice.

distractorNotes.D: PAU have no phone-charging capability — they are emergency communication devices only.

vocabulary.definition: A fixed telephone in a tunnel connected to the safety control room
The central monitoring station that oversees tunnel operations and safety

questionFr: Peut-on faire demi-tour dans un tunnel ?
questionEn: Can you make a U-turn in a tunnel?

A.fr: Oui, s'il n'y a pas de circulation
A.en: Yes, if there is no traffic

B.fr: Oui, en cas de bouchon
B.en: Yes, in case of a traffic jam

C.fr: Non, c'est absolument interdit en toutes circonstances
C.en: No, it is absolutely prohibited under all circumstances

D.fr: Oui, si le tunnel est assez large
D.en: Yes, if the tunnel is wide enough

explanationFr: Le demi-tour en tunnel est ABSOLUMENT interdit en toutes circonstances. En cas de bouchon ou d'incident, restez dans votre véhicule ou évacuez à pied vers l'issue de secours. Faire demi-tour créerait un danger mortel pour vous et les autres usagers.
explanationEn: U-turns in tunnels are ABSOLUTELY prohibited under all circumstances. In case of a traffic jam or incident, stay in your vehicle or evacuate on foot to the emergency exit. Making a U-turn would create a lethal danger for you and other users.
trapNote: Even in a fire or emergency, NEVER turn around. Walk to the nearest exit instead.

distractorNotes.A: Traffic conditions do not change this absolute prohibition.
distractorNotes.B: Traffic jams do NOT justify a U-turn — ever.
distractorNotes.D: Tunnel width is irrelevant — the prohibition is absolute.

vocabulary.definition: Turning a vehicle 180 degrees to travel in the opposite direction

questionFr: Vous êtes bloqué dans un embouteillage en tunnel. Que devez-vous faire ? (Sélectionnez toutes les bonnes réponses)
questionEn: You are stuck in a traffic jam inside a tunnel. What should you do? (Select all correct answers)

A.fr: Couper le moteur si l'arrêt se prolonge pour réduire les émissions polluantes
A.en: Turn off the engine if the stop is prolonged to reduce emissions

B.fr: Écouter la radio sur la fréquence tunnel pour les consignes
B.en: Listen to the radio on the tunnel frequency for instructions

C.fr: Maintenir les distances de sécurité et ne pas quitter le véhicule
C.en: Maintain safety distances and do not leave the vehicle

D.en: Make a U-turn to exit quickly

explanationFr: En cas d'embouteillage en tunnel : coupez le moteur si l'arrêt est prolongé (réduction des polluants), écoutez la fréquence radio du tunnel, maintenez les distances et restez dans votre véhicule. Le demi-tour est TOUJOURS interdit.
explanationEn: In a tunnel traffic jam: turn off the engine if the stop is prolonged (reduces pollutants), listen to the tunnel radio frequency, maintain distances and stay in your vehicle. U-turns are ALWAYS prohibited.
trapNote: Three correct actions plus one absolute prohibition. The demi-tour trap catches students who panic.

distractorNotes.D: U-turns are NEVER permitted in a tunnel, even in an emergency or traffic jam.

vocabulary.definition: A situation where traffic is at a standstill or moving very slowly

questionFr: Que signifient les panneaux à chevrons avant un passage à niveau (150 m, 100 m, 50 m) ?
questionEn: What do the chevron signs before a level crossing (150 m, 100 m, 50 m) mean?

A.fr: Ils indiquent la distance restante avant le passage à niveau : 3 barres = 150 m, 2 barres = 100 m, 1 barre = 50 m
A.en: They indicate the remaining distance to the level crossing: 3 bars = 150 m, 2 bars = 100 m, 1 bar = 50 m

B.fr: Ils indiquent le nombre de voies ferrées
B.en: They indicate the number of railway tracks

C.fr: Ils indiquent la vitesse maximale autorisée
C.en: They indicate the maximum speed allowed

explanationFr: Les balises de signalisation d'approche (J10) comportent des barres obliques (chevrons) : 3 barres à 150 m, 2 barres à 100 m, 1 barre à 50 m du passage à niveau. Elles permettent au conducteur d'évaluer sa distance d'approche et de ralentir progressivement.
explanationEn: The approach warning signs (J10) feature diagonal bars (chevrons): 3 bars at 150 m, 2 bars at 100 m, 1 bar at 50 m from the level crossing. They allow the driver to assess their approach distance and slow down progressively.
trapNote: 3 bars = 150m (furthest), not the closest. Count DOWN as you approach. Some students reverse the logic.

distractorNotes.B: The number of tracks is indicated by the St Andrew's cross (single or double).
distractorNotes.C: These signs indicate distance, not speed.
distractorNotes.D: Every road sign has a legal purpose.

vocabulary.definition: Chevron countdown signs placed at 150m, 100m, and 50m before a level crossing
Angled lines on signs indicating progressive approach distance

questionFr: Quelles affirmations sont vraies concernant les panneaux temporaires à fond jaune ? (Sélectionnez toutes les bonnes réponses)
questionEn: Which statements are true about temporary signs with a yellow background? (Select all correct answers)

A.fr: Ils ont priorité sur les panneaux permanents
A.en: They take priority over permanent signs

B.fr: Ils ont une valeur réglementaire et sont obligatoires
B.en: They have regulatory value and are mandatory

D.fr: Ils sont mis en place lors de travaux ou de déviations
D.en: They are installed during roadworks or diversions

explanationFr: Les panneaux temporaires (fond jaune) ont TOUJOURS priorité sur les panneaux permanents, ont une valeur réglementaire obligatoire, et sont mis en place lors de travaux ou de déviations. En cas de contradiction avec un panneau permanent, suivez le panneau jaune.
explanationEn: Temporary signs (yellow background) ALWAYS take priority over permanent signs, have mandatory regulatory value, and are installed during roadworks or diversions. In case of contradiction with a permanent sign, follow the yellow sign.
trapNote: Three correct answers — students often only pick one. The key insight is that temporary signs are BOTH regulatory AND have priority.

distractorNotes.C: They apply at all times while installed, not just at night.

vocabulary.definition: Signs with yellow backgrounds installed for roadworks, diversions, or temporary hazards
Standard road signs on white or blue backgrounds that apply under normal conditions

questionFr: Que signifient les différentes couleurs des panneaux directionnels ? (Sélectionnez toutes les bonnes réponses)
questionEn: What do the different colours of directional signs mean? (Select all correct answers)

A.fr: Bleu = direction vers l'autoroute

B.fr: Vert = direction vers une route nationale ou un itinéraire important
B.en: Green = direction to a national road or major route

explanationFr: Code couleur des panneaux directionnels : Bleu = autoroute, Vert = route nationale ou itinéraire interurbain important, Blanc = route locale/communale/départementale, Jaune = déviation temporaire. Cette convention est constante sur tout le réseau français.
explanationEn: Directional sign colour code: Blue = motorway, Green = national road or major inter-urban route, White = local/municipal/departmental road, Yellow = temporary diversion. This convention is consistent across the entire French network.
trapNote: ALL FOUR are correct — this tests whether students know the complete colour code. Most remember blue and green but forget white and yellow.

vocabulary.definition: A sign indicating the direction and distance to destinations
An alternative route around a road closure or works zone

questionFr: Que sont les panneaux à messages variables (PMV) sur autoroute ?
questionEn: What are variable message signs (VMS) on motorways?

A.fr: Des écrans électroniques affichant des informations en temps réel sur le trafic, la météo et les dangers
A.en: Electronic screens displaying real-time information about traffic, weather and hazards

B.fr: Des panneaux publicitaires

D.fr: Des panneaux indiquant uniquement les péages

explanationFr: Les PMV (Panneaux à Messages Variables) sont des afficheurs électroniques gérés par les centres de gestion du trafic. Ils informent en temps réel : bouchons, accidents, vitesses recommandées, fermetures de voies, conditions météo. Leurs indications doivent être suivies.
explanationEn: VMS (Variable Message Signs) are electronic displays managed by traffic management centres. They provide real-time information: traffic jams, accidents, recommended speeds, lane closures, weather conditions. Their instructions must be followed.
trapNote: PMV instructions have regulatory value — if they show a speed limit, it must be obeyed even without a physical sign.

distractorNotes.B: Advertising on motorways is separate from PMV — they serve safety functions.
distractorNotes.C: They are functional safety devices, not decorative.
distractorNotes.D: They display a wide range of information, not just toll data.

vocabulary.definition: An electronic motorway sign that displays changing information about traffic and safety

questionFr: Quelles affirmations sont correctes concernant les bornes kilométriques ? (Sélectionnez toutes les bonnes réponses)
questionEn: Which statements are correct about kilometre markers? (Select all correct answers)

A.fr: Rouge = route nationale (N)

B.fr: Jaune = route départementale (D)

D.fr: Elles servent aussi à localiser précisément un incident pour les secours
D.en: They also serve to precisely locate an incident for emergency services

explanationFr: Les bornes kilométriques indiquent la catégorie de la route : rouge (N) = route nationale, jaune (D) = route départementale, bleue (A) = autoroute. Elles servent aussi de repère de localisation pour les secours en cas d'accident.
explanationEn: Kilometre markers indicate the road category: red (N) = national road, yellow (D) = departmental road, blue (A) = motorway. They also serve as location references for emergency services in case of accident.
trapNote: Three correct answers. The location function is often overlooked — these markers help emergency services find you precisely.

distractorNotes.C: These are road category indicators, not hazard warnings — red and yellow have no danger meaning here.

vocabulary.definition: A small post on the roadside indicating the distance along the route and the road category
A major road managed by the state, designated with N and a number
A road managed by the département, designated with D and a number

questionFr: Que signifient les couleurs des marquages au sol ? (Sélectionnez toutes les bonnes réponses)
questionEn: What do the colours of road markings mean? (Select all correct answers)

A.fr: Blanc = marquages permanents

B.fr: Jaune = marquages temporaires (travaux)

D.fr: Ligne discontinue = franchissement autorisé sous conditions
D.en: Dashed line = crossing allowed under conditions

explanationFr: Marquages au sol : blanc = permanent, jaune = temporaire (priorité sur le blanc en cas de travaux). Ligne continue = interdiction de franchir/chevaucher. Ligne discontinue = franchissement autorisé pour dépasser ou changer de voie si la sécurité le permet.
explanationEn: Road markings: white = permanent, yellow = temporary (takes priority over white during works). Continuous line = no crossing/straddling. Dashed line = crossing allowed for overtaking or lane changes if safety permits.
trapNote: ALL FOUR are correct — this is a comprehensive knowledge check on road marking rules.

vocabulary.definition: Lines and symbols painted on the road surface to guide and regulate traffic
An unbroken line that must not be crossed or straddled
A line with gaps that may be crossed when safe

questionFr: À quoi servent les flèches directionnelles peintes au sol ?
questionEn: What are the directional arrows painted on the road surface for?

A.fr: Elles indiquent la direction obligatoire ou le présélection de voie
A.en: They indicate mandatory direction or lane pre-selection

B.fr: Elles sont purement décoratives

explanationFr: Les flèches au sol de présélection indiquent aux conducteurs la voie à emprunter selon leur destination (tout droit, gauche, droite). Elles deviennent obligatoires lorsqu'elles sont entourées d'une ligne continue. Changer de voie après une flèche de présélection est interdit.
explanationEn: Lane pre-selection arrows on the road indicate which lane drivers should use based on their destination (straight, left, right). They become mandatory when bordered by a continuous line. Changing lanes after a pre-selection arrow is prohibited.
trapNote: Once you are in a lane with a directional arrow and a continuous line, you MUST follow that direction — no lane changes.

distractorNotes.B: Road markings always have a regulatory or informational purpose.
distractorNotes.C: One-way streets are indicated by signs, not just arrows.
distractorNotes.D: These arrows apply to all road users, not just buses.

vocabulary.definition: An arrow on the road surface indicating the direction assigned to that lane

questionFr: Comment sont matérialisés les passages piétons en France ?
questionEn: How are pedestrian crossings marked in France?

A.fr: Par des bandes blanches rectangulaires parallèles (zébra)

B.fr: Par des pointillés rouges

C.fr: Par des lignes jaunes en zigzag

D.fr: Par des cercles peints au sol
D.en: By circles painted on the ground

explanationFr: Les passages piétons sont matérialisés par des bandes blanches rectangulaires parallèles à l'axe de la route (marquage zébré). Les piétons engagés sur un passage piétons ont toujours la priorité absolue.
explanationEn: Pedestrian crossings are marked by white rectangular stripes parallel to the road axis (zebra marking). Pedestrians on a pedestrian crossing always have absolute priority.
trapNote: A basic question but important — the zebra pattern is universal in France. Yellow zigzag lines are UK school markings, not French.

distractorNotes.B: Red dotted lines do not exist in French road markings.
distractorNotes.C: Yellow zigzag lines are used in some other countries, not France.
distractorNotes.D: Circles are not used for pedestrian crossing markings.

vocabulary.definition: A marked area of road where pedestrians have priority to cross
Alternating white stripes that mark a pedestrian crossing

questionFr: À quoi servent les bandes rugueuses (ou bandes sonores) au bord de la route ou en travers ?
questionEn: What are rumble strips (or raised markings) at the road edge or across the road for?

A.fr: Alerter le conducteur par des vibrations et du bruit qu'il quitte sa voie ou qu'il approche d'un danger
A.en: Alert the driver through vibrations and noise that they are leaving their lane or approaching a danger

B.fr: Améliorer l'esthétique de la route

explanationFr: Les bandes rugueuses (ou bandes d'alerte sonore) sont des surépaisseurs sur la chaussée qui provoquent des vibrations et du bruit au passage d'un véhicule. Elles alertent les conducteurs inattentifs ou somnolents d'un déport de voie, ou signalent l'approche d'un point dangereux.
explanationEn: Rumble strips are raised markings on the road surface that cause vibrations and noise when driven over. They alert inattentive or drowsy drivers of lane departure, or signal the approach of a danger point.
trapNote: Rumble strips serve as a physical 'wake-up call' — they are a safety feature, not a traffic calming measure.

distractorNotes.B: Road features always serve a safety or regulatory purpose.
distractorNotes.C: Rumble strips affect all vehicles equally.
distractorNotes.D: Municipal boundaries are indicated by signs, not road markings.

vocabulary.definition: A raised strip on the road surface that creates vibration and noise as a warning
Another name for rumble strips, emphasising their noise-producing function

questionFr: Que signifient les panneaux d'altitude en montagne ?
questionEn: What do altitude signs in mountains indicate?

A.fr: L'altitude du point où le panneau est placé, utile pour anticiper les conditions météo
A.en: The altitude of the point where the sign is placed, useful for anticipating weather conditions

B.fr: La vitesse maximale autorisée en mètres

C.en: The distance to the next village

explanationFr: Les panneaux d'altitude (E34) indiquent l'élévation en mètres au-dessus du niveau de la mer. Ils aident les conducteurs à anticiper les conditions : risque de verglas, neige, brouillard. Plus l'altitude est élevée, plus la prudence est nécessaire.
explanationEn: Altitude signs (E34) indicate the elevation in metres above sea level. They help drivers anticipate conditions: risk of black ice, snow, fog. The higher the altitude, the more caution is needed.
trapNote: The number on the sign is metres of altitude, not speed or distance. Context matters — these appear in mountain areas.

distractorNotes.B: Speed is shown on round signs with a red border, not on altitude panels.
distractorNotes.C: Distance to villages is shown on directional signs, not altitude panels.
distractorNotes.D: Road numbers appear on different types of signs.

vocabulary.definition: A sign showing the height above sea level at that point on the road
A thin, transparent layer of ice on the road surface, extremely dangerous

questionFr: Quelle est la signification des panneaux d'information touristique de couleur marron ?
questionEn: What is the meaning of brown-coloured tourist information signs?

A.fr: Ils indiquent des sites touristiques, culturels ou de loisirs à proximité
A.en: They indicate tourist, cultural or leisure sites nearby

B.fr: Ils indiquent des routes en terre

explanationFr: Les panneaux à fond marron (H) sont des panneaux d'information touristique. Ils signalent des monuments historiques, sites naturels, musées, parcs de loisirs et autres points d'intérêt touristique. Ils n'imposent aucune obligation particulière au conducteur.
explanationEn: Brown-background signs (H series) are tourist information signs. They indicate historic monuments, natural sites, museums, leisure parks and other tourist points of interest. They impose no particular obligation on the driver.
trapNote: Brown = tourism. Not to be confused with green (national roads) or blue (motorways). These signs are informational only.

distractorNotes.B: Unpaved roads have specific warning signs, not brown panels.
distractorNotes.C: Agricultural zones are not indicated by brown signs.
distractorNotes.D: Private roads have separate access restriction signs.

vocabulary.definition: A brown-background sign indicating a point of tourist or cultural interest

questionFr: Que signifie le marquage CE (Conformité Européenne) sur les équipements routiers et de sécurité ?
questionEn: What does the CE (Conformité Européenne) marking on road and safety equipment mean?

A.fr: L'équipement est conforme aux normes européennes de sécurité et peut être commercialisé dans l'UE
A.en: The equipment complies with European safety standards and can be sold in the EU

B.fr: L'équipement est fabriqué en France
B.en: The equipment is made in France

explanationFr: Le marquage CE (Conformité Européenne) signifie que le produit respecte les directives européennes applicables en matière de sécurité. Pour les équipements automobiles (gilet, triangle, extincteur, siège enfant), le marquage CE est obligatoire pour la vente et l'utilisation dans l'UE.
explanationEn: The CE marking (Conformité Européenne) means the product meets applicable European directives for safety. For automotive equipment (vest, triangle, fire extinguisher, child seat), the CE marking is mandatory for sale and use in the EU.
trapNote: CE does NOT mean 'made in Europe' — it means the product meets European safety STANDARDS. It could be manufactured anywhere.

distractorNotes.B: CE indicates compliance with standards, not country of manufacture.
distractorNotes.C: CE has nothing to do with price.
distractorNotes.D: Recyclability is indicated by different symbols.

vocabulary.definition: A certification mark indicating conformity with European health, safety, and environmental standards
A technical specification adopted by a European standards organisation

questionFr: Quelles affirmations sont correctes concernant l'assurance automobile obligatoire en France ? (Sélectionnez toutes les bonnes réponses)
questionEn: Which statements are correct about mandatory car insurance in France? (Select all correct answers)

A.fr: L'assurance responsabilité civile (au tiers) est la seule assurance obligatoire
A.en: Third-party liability insurance is the only mandatory insurance

B.fr: Elle couvre les dommages causés à autrui
B.en: It covers damages caused to others

D.fr: Conduire sans assurance est un délit passible de 3 750 € d'amende
D.en: Driving without insurance is a criminal offence punishable by a EUR 3,750 fine

explanationFr: L'assurance responsabilité civile (au tiers) est la seule assurance obligatoire (art. L211-1 du Code des assurances). Elle couvre les dommages causés à autrui (pas votre propre véhicule). Conduire sans assurance est un délit passible de 3 750 € d'amende.
explanationEn: Third-party liability insurance is the only mandatory insurance (Article L211-1 of the Insurance Code). It covers damages caused to others (not your own vehicle). Driving without insurance is a criminal offence punishable by a EUR 3,750 fine.
trapNote: Three correct answers. Students often miss the severity of driving uninsured — it is a criminal offence, not just a fine.

distractorNotes.C: Comprehensive insurance (tous risques) is recommended but NOT legally required.

vocabulary.definition: Insurance covering damages you cause to other people and their property
The minimum mandatory insurance covering only damages to others
Full insurance covering damages to yourself and your vehicle as well as to others

questionFr: Comment remplir un constat amiable ? (Sélectionnez toutes les bonnes réponses)
questionEn: How do you fill out an accident report form (constat amiable)? (Select all correct answers)

A.fr: Le remplir avec l'autre conducteur, sur place, avec un stylo à bille
A.en: Fill it out with the other driver, on site, with a ballpoint pen

B.fr: Dessiner un croquis précis de l'accident
B.en: Draw a precise diagram of the accident

C.fr: Le signer par les deux conducteurs avant de détacher les exemplaires
C.en: Have both drivers sign before separating the copies

D.fr: Le remplir seul à la maison et l'envoyer par courrier
D.en: Fill it out alone at home and send by mail

explanationFr: Le constat amiable doit être : 1) rempli sur place avec l'autre conducteur, au stylo à bille (pas au crayon). 2) Comporter un croquis précis. 3) Être signé par les DEUX conducteurs avant de séparer les feuillets autocopiants. Une fois séparé, aucune modification n'est possible.
explanationEn: The accident report form must be: 1) filled out on site with the other driver, in ballpoint pen (not pencil). 2) Include a precise diagram. 3) Be signed by BOTH drivers before separating the carbon copies. Once separated, no modifications are possible.
trapNote: THREE key rules: on site + together, diagram, dual signature BEFORE separation. Filling it at home defeats the purpose.

distractorNotes.D: The form must be completed on the spot with both drivers present — completing it at home invalidates the process.

vocabulary.definition: A standardized two-part form filled by both drivers at an accident scene
A simple drawing showing the positions and movements of vehicles during the accident
Layered pages that create a duplicate when written on

questionFr: Combien de points perd-on pour les infractions suivantes : téléphone au volant, feu rouge grillé, conduite en état d'ivresse ?
questionEn: How many points are lost for: using phone while driving, running a red light, drink driving?

A.fr: Téléphone = 3 points, feu rouge = 4 points, alcool = 6 points
A.en: Phone = 3 points, red light = 4 points, alcohol = 6 points

B.fr: Téléphone = 1 point, feu rouge = 2 points, alcool = 4 points
B.en: Phone = 1 point, red light = 2 points, alcohol = 4 points

C.fr: Téléphone = 6 points, feu rouge = 3 points, alcool = 3 points
C.en: Phone = 6 points, red light = 3 points, alcohol = 3 points

explanationFr: Barème de retrait de points : usage du téléphone tenu en main = 3 points (+ 135 €), franchissement d'un feu rouge = 4 points (+ 135 €), conduite en état alcoolique = 6 points (+ amende/prison). Le permis compte 12 points (6 en période probatoire).
explanationEn: Points deduction scale: handheld phone use = 3 points (+ EUR 135), running a red light = 4 points (+ EUR 135), drink driving = 6 points (+ fine/prison). The licence has 12 points (6 during probationary period).
trapNote: Memorise the key numbers: phone=3, red light=4, DUI=6. These are the most commonly tested point values.

distractorNotes.B: These values are too low — the real penalties are more severe.
distractorNotes.C: The hierarchy is wrong — DUI is the most severe, not the phone.
distractorNotes.D: Different infractions carry different point penalties.

vocabulary.definition: The loss of points from a driving licence as a penalty for traffic offences
The French licence system where drivers start with 12 points that decrease with infractions

questionFr: Comment peut-on récupérer des points sur le permis de conduire ? (Sélectionnez toutes les bonnes réponses)
questionEn: How can you recover points on your driving licence? (Select all correct answers)

A.fr: Stage volontaire de sensibilisation = récupération de 4 points (1 stage par an maximum)
A.en: Voluntary awareness course = recovery of 4 points (maximum 1 course per year)

B.fr: Après 3 ans sans infraction avec retrait de points, récupération automatique de la totalité (retour à 12)
B.en: After 3 years without a points-losing offence, automatic full recovery (back to 12)

C.fr: Après 6 mois sans infraction (si perte d'1 seul point), récupération de ce point
C.en: After 6 months without an offence (if only 1 point was lost), recovery of that point

explanationFr: Récupération de points : 1) Stage volontaire = +4 points (max 1/an, ne peut dépasser le plafond de 12). 2) 3 ans sans infraction retrait de points = retour automatique à 12. 3) 6 mois sans infraction quand un seul point a été retiré = récupération de ce point. On ne peut PAS acheter des points.
explanationEn: Points recovery: 1) Voluntary course = +4 points (max once/year, cannot exceed 12 ceiling). 2) 3 years without a points-losing offence = automatic return to 12. 3) 6 months without an offence when only 1 point was lost = recovery of that point. You CANNOT buy points.
trapNote: The 6-month rule ONLY works if you lost exactly 1 point. For 2+ points lost, you need the 3-year clean record or a course.

distractorNotes.D: Points cannot be purchased — this is a common misconception.

vocabulary.definition: A 2-day road safety course that restores up to 4 licence points
The first 3 years (or 2 with AAC) after obtaining a licence, with only 6 points

questionFr: Quelles sont les bonnes pratiques d'éco-conduite ? (Sélectionnez toutes les bonnes réponses)
questionEn: What are good eco-driving practices? (Select all correct answers)

A.fr: Utiliser le frein moteur en descente plutôt que le frein à pied
A.en: Use engine braking downhill rather than the foot brake

B.fr: Vérifier la pression des pneus régulièrement (sous-gonflage = +5% de consommation)
B.en: Check tyre pressure regularly (under-inflation = +5% fuel consumption)

C.fr: Accélérer brutalement pour atteindre rapidement la vitesse de croisière
C.en: Accelerate hard to quickly reach cruising speed

D.fr: Passer les rapports supérieurs dès que possible (avant 2500 tr/min en diesel, 2000 en essence)
D.en: Shift to higher gears as soon as possible (before 2500 rpm diesel, 2000 rpm petrol)

explanationFr: L'éco-conduite comprend : le frein moteur (consommation nulle en décélération), la pression correcte des pneus (sous-gonflage augmente la consommation de 5 à 8%), et le passage rapide aux rapports supérieurs. Les accélérations brutales gaspillent du carburant.
explanationEn: Eco-driving includes: engine braking (zero fuel consumption when decelerating), correct tyre pressure (under-inflation increases consumption by 5-8%), and early gear shifting. Hard acceleration wastes fuel.
trapNote: Engine braking = zero fuel injection in modern cars. This is counter-intuitive but a key eco-driving concept.

distractorNotes.C: Hard acceleration is the OPPOSITE of eco-driving — smooth acceleration is key.

vocabulary.definition: Driving techniques that reduce fuel consumption and emissions
Decelerating by releasing the accelerator while in gear, using the engine's resistance
When tyre pressure is below the recommended level

questionFr: Quel est l'ordre exact du protocole PAS (Protéger, Alerter, Secourir) et que comprend chaque étape ?
questionEn: What is the exact order of the PAS protocol (Protect, Alert, Rescue) and what does each step involve?

A.fr: 1) Protéger (balisage, gilet, triangle à 30m) 2) Alerter (112 ou 15/18, préciser lieu et nombre de victimes) 3) Secourir (gestes de premiers secours, ne pas déplacer sauf danger vital)
A.en: 1) Protect (cordon off, vest, triangle at 30m) 2) Alert (112 or 15/18, specify location and number of victims) 3) Rescue (first aid, do not move unless life-threatening danger)

B.fr: 1) Secourir 2) Alerter 3) Protéger

explanationFr: PAS est un acronyme dans l'ordre : Protéger (gilet, triangle à 30 m minimum, allumer feux de détresse), Alerter (appeler le 112 en donnant le lieu précis, nombre de victimes et leur état), Secourir (premiers soins SANS déplacer la victime sauf danger imminent comme un incendie).
explanationEn: PAS is an acronym in order: Protect (vest, triangle at minimum 30 m, turn on hazard lights), Alert (call 112 giving precise location, number of victims and their condition), Rescue (first aid WITHOUT moving the victim unless imminent danger like fire).
trapNote: The ORDER matters — P then A then S. You must secure the scene BEFORE alerting, and alert BEFORE providing first aid.

distractorNotes.B: Rescuing before protecting puts everyone in danger.
distractorNotes.C: You must protect the scene before making a call.
distractorNotes.D: You must alert professionals before attempting first aid — they may arrive while you stabilise.

vocabulary.definition: The mandatory French emergency response protocol in strict order
The simultaneous flashing of all four indicators to signal an emergency
A reflective triangle placed at least 30m behind a stopped vehicle

questionFr: Quelles affirmations sont correctes concernant le système eCall ? (Sélectionnez toutes les bonnes réponses)
questionEn: Which statements are correct about the eCall system? (Select all correct answers)

A.fr: Il est obligatoire dans les véhicules neufs vendus dans l'UE depuis mars 2018
A.en: It has been mandatory in new vehicles sold in the EU since March 2018

B.fr: Il appelle automatiquement le 112 en cas d'accident grave (déclenchement des airbags)
B.en: It automatically calls 112 in case of a serious accident (airbag deployment)

C.fr: C'est un système de navigation GPS
C.en: It is a GPS navigation system

D.fr: Il transmet la localisation GPS, le sens de circulation et le type de véhicule
D.en: It transmits GPS location, direction of travel and vehicle type

explanationFr: Le système eCall est obligatoire dans les véhicules neufs UE depuis le 31 mars 2018. En cas d'accident grave (détecté par les airbags), il appelle automatiquement le 112 et transmet la localisation GPS, le sens de circulation et le type de véhicule. Il peut aussi être déclenché manuellement.
explanationEn: The eCall system has been mandatory in new EU vehicles since March 31, 2018. In case of a serious accident (detected by airbags), it automatically calls 112 and transmits GPS location, direction of travel and vehicle type. It can also be triggered manually.
trapNote: Three correct answers. eCall uses GPS for location but is NOT a navigation system — it is an emergency system.

distractorNotes.C: eCall is an emergency system, not navigation — though it uses GPS for location transmission.

vocabulary.definition: An automatic emergency call system that contacts 112 after a serious accident
An inflatable safety device that deploys in a collision to protect occupants

questionFr: Quelles sont les règles du contrôle technique (CT) pour un véhicule particulier ?
questionEn: What are the rules for the technical inspection (CT) for a private vehicle?

A.fr: Premier CT dans les 6 mois avant le 4e anniversaire de la première mise en circulation, puis tous les 2 ans
A.en: First CT within 6 months before the 4th anniversary of first registration, then every 2 years

B.fr: Premier CT à 2 ans, puis tous les ans
B.en: First CT at 2 years, then every year

C.fr: Premier CT à 5 ans, puis tous les 3 ans
C.en: First CT at 5 years, then every 3 years

D.fr: Le contrôle technique n'est pas obligatoire
D.en: The technical inspection is not mandatory

explanationFr: Le premier contrôle technique doit être effectué dans les 6 mois précédant le 4e anniversaire de la première mise en circulation du véhicule, puis tous les 2 ans. En cas de contre-visite, le propriétaire dispose de 2 mois pour effectuer les réparations et se présenter à nouveau.
explanationEn: The first technical inspection must be done within 6 months before the vehicle's 4th anniversary of first registration, then every 2 years. In case of a re-inspection, the owner has 2 months to make repairs and present the vehicle again.
trapNote: 4 years for the first, then every 2 years. Students often confuse with other countries' schedules.

distractorNotes.B: 2 years and annual is incorrect — it's 4 years then biennial.
distractorNotes.C: 5 years and 3-year intervals are incorrect.
distractorNotes.D: The CT is absolutely mandatory for all vehicles over 4 years old.

vocabulary.definition: A mandatory periodic check of a vehicle's safety and environmental compliance
A follow-up inspection required when the initial CT reveals serious defects
The date a vehicle was first officially registered for road use

questionFr: Quels documents devez-vous obligatoirement avoir à bord de votre véhicule ? (Sélectionnez toutes les bonnes réponses)
questionEn: What documents must you have in your vehicle at all times? (Select all correct answers)

A.fr: Le permis de conduire

B.fr: Le certificat d'immatriculation (carte grise)

explanationFr: Les documents obligatoires à bord : 1) Permis de conduire (ou récépissé si en cours de renouvellement), 2) Certificat d'immatriculation (carte grise), 3) Attestation d'assurance en cours de validité. Le passeport n'est pas requis pour conduire. La vignette d'assurance n'est plus obligatoire sur le pare-brise depuis 2024.
explanationEn: Mandatory documents on board: 1) Driving licence (or receipt if being renewed), 2) Registration certificate (carte grise), 3) Valid insurance certificate. A passport is not required for driving. The insurance sticker on the windshield is no longer mandatory since 2024.
trapNote: Three documents: licence, registration, insurance. The windshield insurance sticker was abolished in 2024 — only the certificate matters now.

distractorNotes.D: A passport or ID card is not a mandatory vehicle document — only driving-specific documents are required.

vocabulary.definition: The official document proving a vehicle is registered (commonly called carte grise)
Proof that the vehicle is covered by mandatory third-party insurance

questionFr: En France, quel est le principe du consentement présumé pour le don d'organes ?
questionEn: In France, what is the principle of presumed consent for organ donation?

A.fr: Toute personne décédée est considérée comme donneuse sauf si elle s'y est opposée de son vivant (inscrite au registre national des refus)
A.en: Every deceased person is considered a donor unless they objected during their lifetime (registered in the national refusal registry)

B.fr: Il faut une carte de donneur pour être donneur
B.en: You need a donor card to be a donor

D.fr: Le don d'organes est interdit en France
D.en: Organ donation is prohibited in France

explanationFr: En France, la loi du 22 décembre 1976 (loi Caillavet), renforcée en 2017, pose le principe du consentement présumé : toute personne est donneuse d'organes par défaut. Pour s'y opposer, il faut s'inscrire au Registre National des Refus ou avoir exprimé son refus de son vivant à ses proches.
explanationEn: In France, the law of December 22, 1976 (Caillavet law), reinforced in 2017, establishes presumed consent: everyone is an organ donor by default. To opt out, one must register with the National Refusal Registry or have expressed refusal to family members during their lifetime.
trapNote: France uses OPT-OUT, not opt-in. You are a donor UNLESS you actively refuse. This surprises many exam candidates.

distractorNotes.B: No donor card is needed — consent is PRESUMED. The card system is opt-in, which is NOT the French model.
distractorNotes.C: The family is consulted but the law favours presumed consent of the deceased.
distractorNotes.D: Organ donation is legal and actively encouraged in France.

vocabulary.definition: The legal assumption that a person consents to organ donation unless they have explicitly refused
The official registry where individuals can register their opposition to organ donation

questionFr: Quelles sont les sanctions pour un conducteur novice (période probatoire) contrôlé avec un taux d'alcoolémie entre 0,2 et 0,5 g/l de sang ?
questionEn: What are the penalties for a novice driver (probationary period) caught with a blood alcohol level between 0.2 and 0.5 g/l?

A.fr: Amende de 135 € et retrait de 6 points

B.fr: Simple avertissement verbal

C.fr: Aucune sanction car le taux est inférieur à 0,5 g/l
C.en: No penalty because the level is under 0.5 g/l

D.fr: Amende de 35 € et retrait de 1 point

explanationFr: Pour les conducteurs novices (permis probatoire), le taux maximal autorisé est de 0,2 g/l de sang (contre 0,5 g/l pour les conducteurs expérimentés). Un taux entre 0,2 et 0,5 g/l entraîne une amende de 135 € et un retrait de 6 points. Avec seulement 6 points en probatoire, cela signifie une invalidation immédiate du permis.
explanationEn: For novice drivers (probationary licence), the maximum allowed level is 0.2 g/l blood (vs 0.5 g/l for experienced drivers). A level between 0.2 and 0.5 g/l results in a EUR 135 fine and 6 points deducted. With only 6 points during probation, this means immediate licence invalidation.
trapNote: 0.2 g/l threshold for novice drivers, NOT 0.5 g/l. And losing 6 points = instant licence invalidation since probationary drivers only have 6 points.

distractorNotes.B: Alcohol offences are never treated with just a warning.
distractorNotes.C: The 0.5 g/l limit applies to experienced drivers only — novices have a 0.2 g/l limit.
distractorNotes.D: The penalty is much more severe than a minor fine.

vocabulary.definition: The first years after obtaining a licence (3 years standard, 2 with AAC) with only 6 points
The concentration of alcohol in the blood, measured in grams per litre
Automatic cancellation of the driving licence when all points are lost

questionFr: Dans une zone de rencontre (zone 20), quelle est la vitesse maximale autorisée et qui est prioritaire ?
questionEn: In a shared space (zone 20), what is the maximum speed allowed and who has priority?

A.fr: 20 km/h, les piétons sont prioritaires

B.fr: 30 km/h, les piétons sont prioritaires

explanationFr: La zone de rencontre limite la vitesse à 20 km/h. Les piétons y sont prioritaires et peuvent circuler sur la chaussée.
explanationEn: A shared space (zone de rencontre) limits speed to 20 km/h. Pedestrians have priority and may walk on the roadway.
trapNote: Confusion between zone de rencontre (20 km/h) and zone 30. Both prioritize pedestrians but at different speeds.

distractorNotes.B: 30 km/h is the limit for a zone 30, not a zone de rencontre.
distractorNotes.C: Vehicles are never prioritized over pedestrians in a zone de rencontre.
distractorNotes.D: 50 km/h is the default urban speed limit, not applicable in shared spaces.

vocabulary.definition: A low-speed zone where pedestrians have priority over all vehicles, limited to 20 km/h
A zone reserved for pedestrians where vehicles are generally prohibited except for deliveries

questionFr: Quelles sont les limitations de vitesse spécifiques pour un conducteur en période probatoire ?
questionEn: What are the specific speed limits for a driver during the probationary period?

A.fr: 110 km/h sur autoroute, 100 km/h sur voie express, 80 km/h sur route
A.en: 110 km/h on motorway, 100 km/h on expressway, 80 km/h on road

B.fr: 130 km/h sur autoroute, 110 km/h sur voie express, 90 km/h sur route
B.en: 130 km/h on motorway, 110 km/h on expressway, 90 km/h on road

C.fr: 100 km/h sur autoroute, 80 km/h sur voie express, 70 km/h sur route
C.en: 100 km/h on motorway, 80 km/h on expressway, 70 km/h on road

D.fr: 110 km/h sur autoroute, 90 km/h sur voie express, 80 km/h sur route
D.en: 110 km/h on motorway, 90 km/h on expressway, 80 km/h on road

explanationFr: Un conducteur en permis probatoire est limité à 110 km/h sur autoroute, 100 km/h sur les voies express à chaussées séparées, et 80 km/h sur les routes à double sens.
explanationEn: A probationary driver is limited to 110 km/h on motorways, 100 km/h on dual carriageway expressways, and 80 km/h on two-way roads.
trapNote: Many confuse the expressway limit (100 km/h) with the motorway limit (110 km/h) for probationary drivers.

distractorNotes.B: These are limits for experienced drivers, not probationary ones.
distractorNotes.C: These limits are too restrictive and do not correspond to any French regulation.
distractorNotes.D: The expressway limit for probationary drivers is 100 km/h, not 90 km/h.

vocabulary.definition: A restricted license given to new drivers for 3 years (or 2 with accompanied driving)
A dual carriageway road with limited access, speed limit of 110 km/h for normal drivers

questionFr: Sur une voie d'accélération d'autoroute, que devez-vous faire ?
questionEn: On a motorway acceleration lane, what must you do?

A.fr: Accélérer pour atteindre la vitesse du trafic et s'insérer en cédant le passage
A.en: Accelerate to match traffic speed and merge while yielding

B.fr: S'arrêter au bout de la voie et attendre un espace libre
B.en: Stop at the end of the lane and wait for a gap

C.en: Drive slowly and force your way in

D.fr: Utiliser la bande d'arrêt d'urgence si la voie est pleine
D.en: Use the hard shoulder if the lane is full

explanationFr: La voie d'accélération sert à atteindre progressivement la vitesse du flux de circulation sur l'autoroute. Vous devez céder le passage aux véhicules déjà sur l'autoroute.
explanationEn: The acceleration lane is designed to gradually reach the speed of motorway traffic. You must yield to vehicles already on the motorway.
trapNote: Some think they have priority when merging — they do not. Motorway traffic has priority.

distractorNotes.B: Stopping on an acceleration lane is extremely dangerous and prohibited.
distractorNotes.C: Forcing insertion at low speed creates a serious collision risk.
distractorNotes.D: The hard shoulder is reserved for emergencies only, never for merging.

vocabulary.definition: A lane at motorway entries allowing vehicles to reach traffic speed before merging
The emergency stopping lane on the right side of a motorway

questionFr: Quand devez-vous allumer vos feux de croisement en journée ?
questionEn: When must you turn on your dipped headlights during the day?

A.fr: En cas de pluie, brouillard ou visibilité réduite
A.en: In rain, fog, or reduced visibility

B.fr: Uniquement dans les tunnels

C.en: Never, daytime running lights are sufficient

explanationFr: Les feux de croisement doivent être allumés dès que la visibilité est insuffisante : pluie, brouillard, neige, ou dans un tunnel. Les feux diurnes ne suffisent pas dans ces conditions.
explanationEn: Dipped headlights must be turned on whenever visibility is insufficient: rain, fog, snow, or in a tunnel. Daytime running lights are not enough in these conditions.
trapNote: Daytime running lights (DRL) only illuminate the front and are not visible from behind — insufficient in poor weather.

distractorNotes.B: Tunnels require dipped headlights, but they are also required in any reduced visibility condition.
distractorNotes.C: DRL do not illuminate the rear of the vehicle, making it invisible to following traffic in poor weather.
distractorNotes.D: The obligation applies on all road types when visibility is reduced.

vocabulary.definition: Low-beam headlights that illuminate the road without blinding oncoming traffic
Front-only lights that turn on automatically to make the vehicle visible during the day

questionFr: Sur une route à double sens avec une voie réversible signalée par des feux, que signifie un feu jaune clignotant au-dessus de la voie ?
questionEn: On a two-way road with a reversible lane indicated by overhead signals, what does a flashing yellow light above the lane mean?

A.fr: La voie va changer de sens, évacuez-la rapidement
A.en: The lane is about to change direction, vacate it quickly

B.fr: La voie est ouverte dans votre sens
B.en: The lane is open in your direction

C.fr: La voie est fermée à la circulation
C.en: The lane is closed to traffic

explanationFr: Un feu jaune clignotant au-dessus d'une voie réversible signifie que la voie va bientôt changer d'affectation. Vous devez l'évacuer dès que possible et rejoindre une voie autorisée.
explanationEn: A flashing yellow light above a reversible lane means the lane is about to change direction. You must vacate it as soon as possible and move to a permitted lane.
trapNote: A green arrow means open, red X means closed, flashing yellow means transition — must leave immediately.

distractorNotes.B: A green downward arrow indicates the lane is open in your direction, not a flashing yellow.
distractorNotes.C: A red X indicates the lane is closed. Flashing yellow means it is transitioning.
distractorNotes.D: This is not a general hazard warning but a specific lane-direction change signal.

vocabulary.definition: A lane whose traffic direction can change based on time of day or traffic conditions
Overhead signals (green arrow, red X, yellow flash) indicating whether a lane is open, closed, or transitioning

questionFr: À l'approche d'une barrière de péage, quelles précautions devez-vous prendre ?
questionEn: When approaching a toll barrier, what precautions must you take?

A.fr: Ralentir progressivement et choisir sa voie à l'avance
A.en: Slow down gradually and choose your lane in advance

B.fr: Maintenir la vitesse et freiner au dernier moment
B.en: Maintain speed and brake at the last moment

C.fr: Doubler les véhicules lents pour gagner du temps
C.en: Overtake slow vehicles to save time

D.fr: Changer de voie au dernier moment pour trouver la file la plus courte
D.en: Change lanes at the last moment to find the shortest queue

explanationFr: À l'approche d'un péage, il faut anticiper en ralentissant progressivement et en choisissant sa voie (télépéage, carte, espèces) bien à l'avance pour éviter les manœuvres dangereuses.
explanationEn: When approaching a toll, you must anticipate by gradually slowing down and choosing your lane (electronic pass, card, cash) well in advance to avoid dangerous maneuvers.
trapNote: Last-second lane changes at toll plazas are a major cause of side-impact collisions.

distractorNotes.B: Late braking near toll barriers is dangerous due to vehicles stopping ahead.
distractorNotes.C: Overtaking near toll areas with converging traffic is extremely hazardous.
distractorNotes.D: Last-moment lane changes cause collisions and are prohibited near toll plazas.

vocabulary.definition: A point on a motorway where drivers must pay a fee to continue
An automatic payment system allowing vehicles to pass through toll gates without stopping

questionFr: De nuit, sur une route non éclairée, quand devez-vous passer des feux de route aux feux de croisement ?
questionEn: At night, on an unlit road, when must you switch from full beam to dipped headlights?

A.fr: En croisant ou en suivant un véhicule
A.en: When meeting or following a vehicle

B.fr: Uniquement en croisant un véhicule
B.en: Only when meeting an oncoming vehicle

C.fr: Jamais si la route est non éclairée
C.en: Never if the road is unlit

explanationFr: Vous devez basculer en feux de croisement dès que vous risquez d'éblouir un autre usager, que ce soit un véhicule venant en face ou un véhicule que vous suivez (via le rétroviseur).
explanationEn: You must switch to dipped headlights whenever you risk dazzling another road user, whether an oncoming vehicle or one you are following (through their mirror).
trapNote: Many forget that full beam also blinds the driver ahead through their rearview mirror.

distractorNotes.B: You must also dip when following a vehicle, as full beam reflects in their mirrors.
distractorNotes.C: An unlit road does not exempt you from dipping headlights when other vehicles are present.
distractorNotes.D: In built-up areas full beam is generally prohibited entirely; the rule applies everywhere.

vocabulary.definition: High-beam headlights providing maximum forward visibility on unlit roads
To impair another driver's vision with excessive light

questionFr: Quelles règles s'appliquent au dépassement sur une route à deux voies dans le même sens avec ligne continue à gauche ?
questionEn: What rules apply to overtaking on a two-lane same-direction road with a solid line on the left?

A.fr: Le dépassement est interdit par la gauche
A.en: Overtaking on the left is prohibited

B.fr: Le dépassement est autorisé si le véhicule devant roule à moins de 50 km/h
B.en: Overtaking is allowed if the vehicle ahead is going under 50 km/h

C.fr: Le dépassement par la droite est autorisé dans ce cas
C.en: Overtaking on the right is allowed in this case

D.fr: On peut franchir la ligne continue pour dépasser un vélo
D.en: You may cross the solid line to overtake a bicycle

explanationFr: Une ligne continue interdit formellement son franchissement. Le dépassement par la gauche est donc impossible. Le dépassement par la droite reste interdit sauf en cas de files parallèles au pas.
explanationEn: A solid line strictly prohibits crossing. Overtaking on the left is therefore impossible. Overtaking on the right remains prohibited except in slow-moving parallel queues.
trapNote: Even cyclists cannot justify crossing a solid line — you must wait until the marking changes to a broken line.

distractorNotes.B: No speed threshold allows crossing a solid line for overtaking.
distractorNotes.C: Overtaking on the right is generally prohibited regardless of left-side markings.
distractorNotes.D: Since 2015 you may straddle a broken line for cyclists but never cross a solid continuous line.

vocabulary.definition: A continuous road marking that prohibits crossing or straddling
The act of passing another vehicle moving in the same direction

questionFr: Que signifie un panneau rectangulaire bleu avec une flèche blanche pointant vers le haut dans un sens unique ?
questionEn: What does a rectangular blue sign with a white arrow pointing up mean on a one-way street?

A.fr: Sens unique, circulation dans la direction indiquée
A.en: One-way street, traffic flows in the indicated direction

B.fr: Obligation d'aller tout droit au prochain carrefour
B.en: Obligation to go straight at the next junction

explanationFr: Ce panneau (C12) indique un sens unique. Tous les véhicules doivent circuler dans la direction de la flèche. Il ne faut pas le confondre avec le panneau d'obligation de direction (rond, fond bleu).
explanationEn: This sign (C12) indicates a one-way street. All vehicles must travel in the arrow's direction. Do not confuse it with the mandatory direction sign (round, blue background).
trapNote: The rectangular shape distinguishes it from the round mandatory direction sign — round means obligation at junction, rectangular means one-way.

distractorNotes.B: The obligation to go straight is shown by a round sign with blue background, not rectangular.
distractorNotes.C: Bus lanes are marked with specific bus pictograms, not a simple arrow.
distractorNotes.D: Priority road signs are diamond-shaped with yellow and white colors.

vocabulary.definition: A road where traffic is permitted in only one direction
A round blue sign prescribing a specific action or direction

questionFr: Combien de points comporte le permis probatoire à l'obtention, et comment évolue-t-il ?
questionEn: How many points does a probationary license start with, and how does it evolve?

A.fr: 6 points, puis 2 points par an sans infraction (3 ans ou 2 ans avec AAC)
A.en: 6 points, then 2 points per year without offense (3 years or 2 years with AAC)

B.fr: 8 points, puis 2 points par an pendant 2 ans
B.en: 8 points, then 2 points per year for 2 years

C.fr: 6 points, puis 4 points par an pendant 3 ans
C.en: 6 points, then 4 points per year for 3 years

D.en: 12 points from the moment the license is obtained

explanationFr: Le permis probatoire démarre à 6 points. Sans infraction, il gagne 2 points par an pendant 3 ans (filière classique) ou 2 ans (conduite accompagnée AAC), pour atteindre 12 points.
explanationEn: The probationary license starts at 6 points. Without offenses, it gains 2 points per year for 3 years (standard path) or 2 years (accompanied driving AAC), reaching 12 points.
trapNote: With AAC the probationary period is 2 years (gaining 3 points/year), not 3 years. Both paths reach 12 points.

distractorNotes.B: The starting balance is 6 points, not 8.
distractorNotes.C: You gain 2 points per year (or 3 with AAC), not 4.
distractorNotes.D: 12 points is the maximum — new drivers start with only 6.

vocabulary.definition: A license with reduced points issued to new drivers for a trial period
A system allowing learners from age 15 to drive with an experienced companion before the exam

questionFr: Quels sont les signes de fatigue au volant qui doivent vous alerter ?
questionEn: What are the warning signs of driver fatigue you should watch for?

A.fr: Bâillements répétés et picotements des yeux

B.fr: Raideur de la nuque et variations involontaires de trajectoire
B.en: Neck stiffness and involuntary lane drifting

C.fr: Difficulté à maintenir une vitesse constante

D.fr: Envie de rouler plus vite pour arriver rapidement
D.en: Desire to drive faster to arrive quickly

explanationFr: Les bâillements, les picotements des yeux, la raideur de la nuque et les écarts de trajectoire sont les signes classiques de fatigue. Il faut s'arrêter immédiatement pour une pause d'au moins 15 à 20 minutes.
explanationEn: Yawning, itchy eyes, neck stiffness, and lane drifting are classic fatigue signs. You must stop immediately for a break of at least 15 to 20 minutes.
trapNote: Speed inconsistency can have many causes. The specific combination of yawning + drifting is the textbook fatigue indicator.

distractorNotes.C: While possible with fatigue, inconsistent speed alone is not a primary warning sign.
distractorNotes.D: Wanting to drive faster is not a recognized fatigue symptom — it may indicate impatience or stress.

vocabulary.definition: A state of near-sleep that severely impairs driving ability
A designated stopping area on motorways for drivers to rest

questionFr: Quelle est la différence entre une suspension et une annulation du permis de conduire ?
questionEn: What is the difference between a license suspension and a license cancellation?

A.fr: La suspension est temporaire, l'annulation oblige à repasser le permis
A.en: Suspension is temporary, cancellation requires retaking the license exam

B.fr: La suspension est judiciaire, l'annulation est administrative
B.en: Suspension is judicial, cancellation is administrative

C.fr: Les deux sont identiques mais avec des durées différentes
C.en: Both are identical but with different durations

D.fr: L'annulation est temporaire, la suspension est définitive
D.en: Cancellation is temporary, suspension is permanent

explanationFr: La suspension interdit temporairement de conduire (administrative ou judiciaire, jusqu'à 5 ans). L'annulation invalide le permis : il faut repasser les examens (code et conduite) après un délai fixé par le juge.
explanationEn: Suspension temporarily prohibits driving (administrative or judicial, up to 5 years). Cancellation invalidates the license: you must retake both theory and practical exams after a court-determined waiting period.
trapNote: Suspension can be both administrative AND judicial. Annulation/cancellation is always judicial.

distractorNotes.B: Suspension can be either administrative or judicial, not exclusively judicial.
distractorNotes.C: They are fundamentally different — one is temporary, the other requires full re-examination.
distractorNotes.D: This reverses the definitions entirely.

vocabulary.definition: Temporary withdrawal of driving privileges for a specified period
Judicial invalidation of a license requiring the driver to retake all exams

questionFr: Quel est le risque principal de laisser tourner le moteur dans un garage fermé ?
questionEn: What is the main risk of leaving the engine running in a closed garage?

A.fr: Intoxication au monoxyde de carbone (CO), potentiellement mortelle

B.fr: Risque d'explosion du réservoir d'essence

explanationFr: Le monoxyde de carbone (CO) est un gaz inodore et incolore produit par la combustion du moteur. Dans un espace fermé, il s'accumule rapidement et peut provoquer la mort en quelques minutes.
explanationEn: Carbon monoxide (CO) is an odorless, colorless gas produced by engine combustion. In an enclosed space, it accumulates rapidly and can cause death within minutes.
trapNote: CO is undetectable by smell — victims lose consciousness before realizing the danger.

distractorNotes.B: Fuel tank explosions from idling are extremely rare and not the primary concern.
distractorNotes.C: Engine overheating is possible but not the critical life-threatening risk in a closed space.
distractorNotes.D: Battery drain occurs when the engine is off with accessories running, not when idling.

vocabulary.definition: A deadly, odorless gas produced by incomplete combustion of fuel
Harmful effects caused by exposure to a toxic substance

questionFr: Que signifie le disque 'A' apposé à l'arrière d'un véhicule ?
questionEn: What does the 'A' disc on the back of a vehicle mean?

A.fr: Le conducteur est en permis probatoire (jeune conducteur)
A.en: The driver has a probationary license (new driver)

B.fr: Le véhicule est en auto-école
B.en: The vehicle is a driving school car

C.fr: Le conducteur est accompagné d'un moniteur
C.en: The driver is accompanied by an instructor

explanationFr: Le disque 'A' (pour Apprenti) est obligatoire à l'arrière du véhicule conduit par un titulaire de permis probatoire. Il informe les autres usagers que le conducteur est soumis à des limitations spécifiques.
explanationEn: The 'A' disc (for Apprenti/Learner) is mandatory on the rear of vehicles driven by probationary license holders. It informs other road users that the driver is subject to specific restrictions.
trapNote: The 'A' stands for Apprenti (learner), not Auto-école. Driving school cars display a different sign on the roof.

distractorNotes.B: Driving school vehicles display a rooftop sign, not an 'A' disc.
distractorNotes.C: Accompanied driving (conduite accompagnée) uses a different sticker showing the AAC logo.
distractorNotes.D: 'A' does not stand for 'automatique' — it means apprenti (learner/probationary).

vocabulary.definition: A mandatory sign placed on vehicles driven by probationary license holders
A driver in the probationary period following license obtention

questionFr: En conduite accompagnée (AAC), quelles sont les conditions requises pour l'accompagnateur ?
questionEn: In accompanied driving (AAC), what conditions must the accompanying driver meet?

A.fr: Titulaire du permis B depuis au moins 5 ans sans annulation ni invalidation
A.en: Holder of a category B license for at least 5 years without cancellation or invalidation

B.fr: Titulaire du permis B depuis au moins 3 ans
B.en: Holder of a category B license for at least 3 years

C.fr: Avoir suivi une formation spéciale de 7 heures
C.en: Must have completed a special 7-hour training

D.fr: Être âgé de moins de 60 ans

explanationFr: L'accompagnateur doit détenir le permis B depuis au moins 5 ans sans interruption, et ne pas avoir subi d'annulation ou d'invalidation du permis. Il n'y a pas de limite d'âge supérieure ni de formation obligatoire.
explanationEn: The accompanying driver must have held a category B license for at least 5 years continuously, with no history of cancellation or invalidation. There is no upper age limit or mandatory training.
trapNote: The requirement is 5 years, not 3. Many candidates confuse this with other minimum experience thresholds.

distractorNotes.B: The minimum is 5 years of license holding, not 3.
distractorNotes.C: No special training is required for the accompanying driver — only experience.
distractorNotes.D: There is no maximum age limit for an accompanying driver.

vocabulary.definition: An experienced driver who supervises a learner during accompanied driving sessions
Loss of a driving license due to total loss of points

questionFr: Après combien de temps de conduite continue est-il recommandé de faire une pause ?
questionEn: After how much continuous driving time is it recommended to take a break?

A.fr: Toutes les 2 heures

B.fr: Toutes les 4 heures

D.fr: Uniquement quand on se sent fatigué

explanationFr: La recommandation officielle est de s'arrêter au moins 15 à 20 minutes toutes les 2 heures de conduite. La fatigue est la première cause de mortalité sur autoroute.
explanationEn: The official recommendation is to stop for at least 15 to 20 minutes every 2 hours of driving. Fatigue is the leading cause of motorway fatalities.
trapNote: The 2-hour rule is a recommendation, not a legal obligation for private drivers — but it is tested on the exam.

distractorNotes.B: 4 hours is far too long — fatigue symptoms typically appear well before this.
distractorNotes.C: 30 minutes is too frequent for normal driving and not the official recommendation.
distractorNotes.D: Waiting until you feel tired is dangerous — fatigue impairs your ability to judge your own state.

vocabulary.definition: A deliberate stop during a journey to combat fatigue
The continuous period spent actively driving a vehicle

questionFr: Quelles sanctions s'appliquent en cas de récidive de conduite sous l'emprise de l'alcool (taux ≥ 0,8 g/l) ?
questionEn: What penalties apply for a repeat offense of driving under the influence of alcohol (level ≥ 0.8 g/l)?

A.fr: Jusqu'à 4 ans de prison et 9 000 € d'amende
A.en: Up to 4 years in prison and €9,000 fine

B.fr: Jusqu'à 2 ans de prison et 4 500 € d'amende
B.en: Up to 2 years in prison and €4,500 fine

D.fr: Amende forfaitaire de 135 € sans retrait de points
D.en: Fixed fine of €135 without point deduction

explanationFr: En récidive de conduite en état d'ivresse (≥ 0,8 g/l), les peines sont doublées : jusqu'à 4 ans d'emprisonnement et 9 000 € d'amende, plus l'annulation du permis et l'obligation d'installer un éthylotest anti-démarrage (EAD).
explanationEn: For a repeat DUI offense (≥ 0.8 g/l), penalties are doubled: up to 4 years imprisonment and €9,000 fine, plus license cancellation and mandatory installation of an alcohol interlock device (EAD).
trapNote: First offense is 2 years/€4,500. Repeat offense doubles both. The EAD requirement is new since 2019.

distractorNotes.B: These are the penalties for a first offense, not a repeat offense.
distractorNotes.C: A DUI offense at ≥ 0.8 g/l results in 6 points lost plus criminal penalties, not just 3 points.
distractorNotes.D: This describes a minor traffic violation, not a serious criminal alcohol offense.

vocabulary.definition: Committing the same or similar offense again after a previous conviction
A device requiring a breath test before the vehicle can start

questionFr: Qui peut être désigné comme conducteur désigné (Sam) lors d'une soirée ?
questionEn: Who can be designated as the sober driver (Sam) for an evening out?

A.fr: Toute personne titulaire du permis qui s'engage à ne pas consommer d'alcool
A.en: Any license holder who commits to not drinking alcohol

B.fr: Uniquement le propriétaire du véhicule

C.fr: Le conducteur le plus expérimenté du groupe
C.en: The most experienced driver in the group

D.fr: Celui qui a le moins bu en fin de soirée
D.en: The person who drank the least at the end of the evening

explanationFr: Le conducteur désigné (Sam) est une personne qui s'engage AVANT la soirée à ne consommer aucune boisson alcoolisée. N'importe quel titulaire du permis peut remplir ce rôle.
explanationEn: The designated driver (Sam) is a person who commits BEFORE the event to consume no alcohol whatsoever. Any license holder can fill this role.
trapNote: Sam must commit BEFORE the event, not be chosen after based on who drank the least.

distractorNotes.B: Any licensed driver can be Sam, not just the vehicle owner.
distractorNotes.C: Experience is irrelevant — the key criterion is zero alcohol consumption.
distractorNotes.D: Choosing the person who drank the least is dangerous — even small amounts impair driving.

vocabulary.definition: A person who agrees in advance to remain sober and drive others home safely
French awareness campaign name for the designated driver concept

questionFr: À quelle fréquence doit-on vérifier la pression des pneus, et dans quelles conditions ?
questionEn: How often should tire pressure be checked, and under what conditions?

A.fr: Au moins une fois par mois, à froid
A.en: At least once a month, when tires are cold

B.fr: Tous les 6 mois, après avoir roulé

D.fr: Une fois par an lors du contrôle technique
D.en: Once a year during the technical inspection

explanationFr: La pression des pneus doit être vérifiée au moins une fois par mois et toujours à froid (véhicule arrêté depuis plus de 2 heures ou ayant roulé moins de 3 km). La chaleur augmente la pression et fausse la mesure.
explanationEn: Tire pressure should be checked at least once a month and always when cold (vehicle stopped for more than 2 hours or driven less than 3 km). Heat increases pressure and gives a false reading.
trapNote: Checking warm tires shows higher pressure than actual — you might think pressure is fine when it is actually too low.

distractorNotes.B: Six months is far too infrequent, and checking after driving gives inaccurate readings.
distractorNotes.C: Monthly checks are necessary even without long trips — tires lose pressure naturally.
distractorNotes.D: Annual checks are grossly insufficient for tire safety.

vocabulary.definition: The amount of air inside a tire, measured in bars or PSI
Tire condition after the vehicle has been stationary for at least 2 hours

questionFr: Quelles sont les règles concernant le dépassement du chargement à l'arrière d'un véhicule léger ?
questionEn: What are the rules regarding load overhang at the rear of a light vehicle?

A.fr: Maximum 3 mètres de dépassement avec signalisation obligatoire
A.en: Maximum 3 meters overhang with mandatory signaling

B.fr: Maximum 1 mètre sans signalisation nécessaire

D.fr: Maximum 5 mètres avec un drapeau rouge
D.en: Maximum 5 meters with a red flag

explanationFr: Un chargement peut dépasser de 3 mètres maximum à l'arrière d'un véhicule léger. Au-delà de 1 mètre, il doit être signalé par un dispositif réfléchissant (panneau carré rouge et blanc) visible de jour comme de nuit.
explanationEn: A load may extend a maximum of 3 meters beyond the rear of a light vehicle. Beyond 1 meter, it must be marked with a reflective device (red and white square panel) visible day and night.
trapNote: Up to 1 meter requires no marking; between 1-3 meters requires reflective signaling; over 3 meters is prohibited.

distractorNotes.B: While under 1 meter needs no signaling, the maximum allowed is 3 meters, not 1.
distractorNotes.C: Rear overhang is permitted up to 3 meters with proper signaling.
distractorNotes.D: 5 meters exceeds the legal maximum, and a flag alone is insufficient — reflective panels are required.

vocabulary.definition: The portion of a load extending beyond the vehicle's bodywork
A panel or strip that reflects light to warn other road users of a protruding load

questionFr: Où doit-on placer le triangle de présignalisation en cas de panne sur une route ?
questionEn: Where should the warning triangle be placed in case of a breakdown on a road?

A.fr: À au moins 30 mètres en amont du véhicule, visible à 100 mètres
A.en: At least 30 meters before the vehicle, visible from 100 meters

B.fr: Directement derrière le véhicule

C.en: On the roof of the vehicle

explanationFr: Le triangle de présignalisation doit être placé à au moins 30 mètres en amont du véhicule immobilisé, de manière à être visible par les conducteurs arrivant de loin (au moins 100 mètres). Attention : sur autoroute, il est déconseillé de le poser (danger piéton).
explanationEn: The warning triangle must be placed at least 30 meters before the stationary vehicle, visible to approaching drivers from at least 100 meters. Note: on motorways, placing it is discouraged due to pedestrian danger.
trapNote: On motorways, do NOT place the triangle — stay behind the barrier and call emergency services instead.

distractorNotes.B: Placing it directly behind gives no advance warning to approaching vehicles.
distractorNotes.C: The triangle must be on the road surface to be effective, not on the vehicle.
distractorNotes.D: 10 meters is insufficient warning distance for approaching traffic.

vocabulary.definition: A reflective triangular device placed on the road to warn of a stationary vehicle ahead
In the direction from which traffic is approaching

questionFr: Quels éléments devez-vous obligatoirement avoir dans votre véhicule en France ?
questionEn: What items must you have in your vehicle in France by law?

A.fr: Un gilet jaune et un triangle de présignalisation
A.en: A high-visibility vest and a warning triangle

B.fr: Un gilet jaune, un triangle et une trousse de secours
B.en: A high-visibility vest, a triangle, and a first aid kit

D.fr: Un extincteur et une trousse de secours
D.en: A fire extinguisher and a first aid kit

explanationFr: En France, tout véhicule doit disposer d'un gilet de haute visibilité (à portée de main du conducteur) et d'un triangle de présignalisation. L'éthylotest n'est plus obligatoire depuis 2020. La trousse de secours est recommandée mais pas obligatoire.
