vocabulary.definition: A warning sign alerting drivers to a hazard ahead on the road
A sign indicating an obligation, prohibition, or restriction

questionFr: En cas de conflit entre la signalisation temporaire et la signalisation permanente, laquelle prévaut ?
questionEn: When temporary signage conflicts with permanent signage, which takes precedence?

A.fr: La signalisation permanente

B.fr: La signalisation temporaire

D.fr: La signalisation la plus récente visuellement

explanationFr: La signalisation temporaire (à fond jaune pour le marquage au sol, ou panneaux temporaires sur pieds) prévaut toujours sur la signalisation permanente. Elle est mise en place pour des travaux, des déviations ou des situations exceptionnelles et doit être respectée en priorité.
explanationEn: Temporary signage (yellow background for road markings, or temporary signs on stands) always takes precedence over permanent signage. It is set up for roadworks, detours, or exceptional situations and must be obeyed as a priority.
trapNote: Some students think 'most restrictive' applies, but the rule is simple: temporary always overrides permanent, even if temporary is LESS restrictive.

distractorNotes.A: Permanent signage is overridden by temporary signage in all cases of conflict.
distractorNotes.C: The hierarchy is based on temporality, not restrictiveness — temporary always wins.
distractorNotes.D: Visual appearance does not determine priority; the legal classification as temporary or permanent does.

vocabulary.definition: Signs and markings installed for a limited time during roadworks or exceptional events
The standard fixed signs and road markings installed for normal traffic conditions

questionFr: Sur les panneaux de jalonnement (directionnels), la couleur bleue indique une direction vers :
questionEn: On directional (jalonnement) signs, the color blue indicates a direction toward:

A.fr: Une route départementale

B.fr: Une route nationale

explanationFr: Le code couleur des panneaux de jalonnement français est : bleu pour les autoroutes, vert pour les routes nationales ou grandes directions, jaune pour les routes départementales, et blanc pour les directions locales.
explanationEn: The French directional sign color code is: blue for motorways, green for national roads or major routes, yellow for departmental roads, and white for local directions.
trapNote: Students often confuse green (national) and blue (motorway). Remember: autoroute = bleu, nationale = vert.

distractorNotes.A: Departmental roads use yellow directional signs.
distractorNotes.B: National roads use green directional signs.
distractorNotes.D: City center directions typically use white signs with a black border.

vocabulary.definition: The system of signs guiding drivers to destinations via colored directional panels
A sign indicating the direction and distance to a destination

questionFr: Les lignes en zigzag jaunes peintes sur la chaussée indiquent :
questionEn: Yellow zigzag lines painted on the road surface indicate:

A.fr: Une zone d'interdiction de dépassement

B.fr: Une zone d'arrêt et de stationnement interdits (arrêt de bus ou livraison)
B.en: A no-stopping and no-parking zone (bus stop or delivery area)

D.fr: Une zone de réduction de vitesse

explanationFr: Les lignes en zigzag jaunes sur la chaussée délimitent une zone où l'arrêt et le stationnement sont interdits. Elles sont utilisées principalement devant les arrêts de bus et les zones de livraison pour garantir un accès libre.
explanationEn: Yellow zigzag lines on the road surface delineate a zone where both stopping and parking are prohibited. They are mainly used in front of bus stops and delivery zones to ensure clear access.
trapNote: Students may think zigzag lines are decorative or related to speed. They specifically mean no stopping AND no parking in that zone.

distractorNotes.A: No-overtaking zones use continuous center lines, not zigzag markings.
distractorNotes.C: Pedestrian crossings use white parallel bands, not zigzag lines.
distractorNotes.D: Speed reduction zones are not marked with zigzag lines.

vocabulary.definition: A jagged road marking indicating a no-stopping zone, typically at bus stops
A designated location where public buses stop to pick up and drop off passengers

questionFr: Que signifie un marquage au sol de couleur jaune en France ?
questionEn: What does yellow road surface marking mean in France?

A.fr: Signalisation permanente standard

B.fr: Signalisation temporaire ou interdiction de stationner

explanationFr: En France, le marquage au sol jaune est utilisé pour deux fonctions : la signalisation temporaire (travaux, déviations) et l'interdiction de stationner ou de s'arrêter (lignes jaunes continues ou zigzag). Le marquage permanent standard est blanc.
explanationEn: In France, yellow road markings are used for two functions: temporary signage (roadworks, detours) and no-parking or no-stopping restrictions (continuous yellow lines or zigzag). Standard permanent markings are white.
trapNote: In some countries, yellow center lines are standard permanent markings. In France, yellow means temporary or restrictive — white is the standard permanent color.

distractorNotes.A: Standard permanent markings in France are white, not yellow.
distractorNotes.C: Taxi-reserved lanes may have specific markings but are not universally indicated by yellow.
distractorNotes.D: School zones use specific signage and sometimes colored pavement, but yellow markings have a broader meaning.

vocabulary.definition: Painted lines and symbols on the road surface providing guidance and regulation
Road surface markings as opposed to vertical signs on posts

questionFr: Les balises J5 (chevrons jaunes et noirs) placées en virage indiquent :
questionEn: J5 delineator posts (yellow and black chevrons) placed on curves indicate:

A.fr: Une zone de travaux

B.fr: Le sens du virage et un danger lié à la courbe
B.en: The direction of the curve and a danger related to it

C.en: A zone where overtaking is permitted

explanationFr: Les balises J5 sont des panneaux à chevrons jaunes et noirs installés dans les virages dangereux. Elles indiquent le sens du virage (les chevrons pointent dans la direction de la courbe) et alertent le conducteur sur la dangerosité du virage.
explanationEn: J5 delineator posts are yellow and black chevron panels installed at dangerous curves. They indicate the direction of the curve (chevrons point in the direction of the turn) and alert the driver to the curve's danger.
trapNote: J5 chevrons are sometimes confused with construction zone markings due to the yellow/black color. They are permanent road furniture indicating dangerous curves.

distractorNotes.A: Construction zones use different temporary signage; J5 posts are permanent installations.
distractorNotes.C: J5 posts warn of danger — they have no relation to overtaking permissions.
distractorNotes.D: Tunnel approaches use different signage, not J5 chevron posts.

vocabulary.definition: A yellow and black chevron panel placed at dangerous curves to indicate turn direction
A V-shaped or arrow pattern pointing in a specific direction

questionFr: Les bornes hectométriques sur le bord de la route permettent de :
questionEn: Hectometric markers on the roadside allow you to:

A.fr: Mesurer votre vitesse

B.fr: Localiser votre position précise pour les secours
B.en: Pinpoint your exact location for emergency services

C.fr: Indiquer la distance au prochain péage
C.en: Show the distance to the next toll booth

explanationFr: Les bornes hectométriques (petits repères tous les 100 mètres) affichent le numéro de la route et le point kilométrique. En cas d'accident ou de panne, elles permettent de communiquer votre position exacte aux services de secours.
explanationEn: Hectometric markers (small posts every 100 meters) display the road number and kilometric point. In case of an accident or breakdown, they allow you to communicate your exact position to emergency services.
trapNote: While you could theoretically use them to estimate speed by timing between markers, their official purpose is localization for emergency reporting.

distractorNotes.A: Speed measurement is not the purpose of hectometric markers; use your speedometer for that.
distractorNotes.C: Toll distances are shown on overhead or roadside information panels, not hectometric markers.
distractorNotes.D: Dangerous zones are indicated by danger signs, not hectometric markers.

vocabulary.definition: A small roadside post placed every 100 meters showing the road number and distance reference
The distance in kilometers from a reference point on a road, used for location identification

questionFr: Un panneau rond à fond bleu avec un symbole blanc indique :
questionEn: A round sign with a blue background and white symbol indicates:

A.fr: Une interdiction

B.fr: Une obligation

explanationFr: Les panneaux ronds à fond bleu avec un symbole blanc indiquent une obligation (par exemple : direction obligatoire, vitesse minimale, piste cyclable obligatoire). Les interdictions sont sur fond blanc avec bordure rouge.
explanationEn: Round signs with a blue background and white symbol indicate an obligation (e.g., mandatory direction, minimum speed, mandatory cycle path). Prohibitions are on a white background with a red border.
trapNote: Blue round = obligation. Red-bordered round = prohibition. Students often reverse these two categories.

distractorNotes.A: Prohibitions use round signs with white background and red border, not blue.
distractorNotes.C: Information signs are typically square or rectangular with blue background.
distractorNotes.D: Danger signs are triangular with red border and white background.

vocabulary.definition: A round blue sign indicating a required action or minimum standard
A round white sign with red border indicating a forbidden action

questionFr: Un panneau de fin de prescription (cercle blanc barré de traits noirs obliques) annule :
questionEn: An end-of-restriction sign (white circle with diagonal black lines) cancels:

A.fr: Uniquement la limitation de vitesse

B.fr: Toutes les interdictions précédemment signalées

explanationFr: Le panneau de fin de toutes les interdictions (rond blanc barré de traits noirs obliques) annule toutes les prescriptions d'interdiction précédentes : limitation de vitesse, interdiction de dépasser, interdiction de klaxonner, etc. Les panneaux spécifiques de fin (ex. fin de limitation à 70) n'annulent que l'interdiction concernée.
explanationEn: The end-of-all-restrictions sign (white circle with diagonal black lines) cancels all previous prohibition prescriptions: speed limits, no-overtaking, no-honking, etc. Specific end signs (e.g., end of 70 km/h limit) only cancel the particular restriction.
trapNote: Students often think this sign only ends the speed limit. The generic end-of-restriction sign cancels ALL active prohibitions at once.

distractorNotes.A: The generic sign cancels all restrictions, not just the speed limit. A specific end-of-speed-limit sign exists for that purpose.
distractorNotes.C: A specific sign exists for ending the overtaking ban alone — the generic sign cancels everything.
distractorNotes.D: This sign cancels prohibitions (interdictions), not obligations. Obligations have their own end signs.

vocabulary.definition: A sign indicating that previously imposed rules no longer apply from that point
A regulatory rule forbidding a specific action on the road

questionFr: Le protocole PAS signifie :
questionEn: The PAS protocol stands for:

A.fr: Prévenir, Agir, Sécuriser

B.fr: Protéger, Alerter, Secourir

explanationFr: Le protocole PAS est l'ordre d'action à suivre en cas d'accident : Protéger (sécuriser la zone), Alerter (appeler les secours), Secourir (porter les premiers soins). Cet ordre est crucial : on protège d'abord pour éviter un sur-accident.
explanationEn: The PAS protocol is the order of action to follow in an accident: Protect (secure the area), Alert (call emergency services), Rescue (provide first aid). This order is crucial: you protect first to prevent a secondary accident.
trapNote: The order P-A-S matters — students must remember to PROTECT first before calling for help or administering aid.

distractorNotes.A: This is not the correct expansion of the PAS acronym.
distractorNotes.C: This is not the correct expansion and misrepresents the protocol steps.
distractorNotes.D: PAS is not an abbreviation for 'Premiers Actes de Secours' — it is an action sequence.

vocabulary.definition: Securing the accident scene to prevent further harm or secondary accidents
Administering first aid to injured persons after the scene is secured

questionFr: En France, quel numéro d'urgence est valable dans tous les pays de l'Union européenne ?
questionEn: In France, which emergency number is valid in all European Union countries?

A.fr: 15 (SAMU)

B.fr: 17 (Police)

explanationFr: Le 112 est le numéro d'urgence européen, gratuit et accessible depuis tout téléphone dans l'ensemble de l'Union européenne. En France, les numéros nationaux sont le 15 (SAMU), 17 (Police) et 18 (Pompiers), mais seul le 112 fonctionne dans toute l'Europe.
explanationEn: 112 is the European emergency number, free and accessible from any phone throughout the European Union. In France, national numbers are 15 (SAMU), 17 (Police), and 18 (Fire), but only 112 works across all of Europe.
trapNote: Students may know 15, 17, and 18 for French emergencies but forget that 112 is the universal EU number — this is what to call when abroad.

distractorNotes.A: 15 is the SAMU number valid only in France.
distractorNotes.B: 17 is the Police number valid only in France.
distractorNotes.C: 18 is the Fire service number valid only in France.

vocabulary.definition: Service d'Aide Médicale Urgente — the French emergency medical response service reached at 15
The pan-EU emergency number 112, accessible free of charge from any phone

questionFr: La vignette Crit'Air permet de :
questionEn: The Crit'Air sticker is used to:

A.fr: Prouver que le contrôle technique est à jour
A.en: Prove the vehicle inspection is up to date

B.fr: Classer le véhicule selon son niveau d'émissions polluantes
B.en: Classify the vehicle by its pollutant emission level

C.fr: Obtenir une réduction sur les péages

explanationFr: La vignette Crit'Air est un certificat qualité de l'air qui classe les véhicules de 0 (électrique) à 5 selon leur niveau d'émissions. Elle est obligatoire pour circuler dans les Zones à Faibles Émissions mobilité (ZFE-m) et lors des pics de pollution avec circulation différenciée.
explanationEn: The Crit'Air sticker is an air quality certificate that classifies vehicles from 0 (electric) to 5 based on their emission levels. It is mandatory for driving in Low Emission Mobility Zones (ZFE-m) and during pollution peaks with differentiated traffic measures.
trapNote: Crit'Air is about emissions classification, not about technical inspection status. These are two completely separate systems.

distractorNotes.A: The contrôle technique (vehicle inspection) has its own separate paperwork and windshield sticker.
distractorNotes.C: Crit'Air does not provide toll discounts; some toll systems offer discounts for eco-vehicles separately.
distractorNotes.D: Vehicle owner identification is done through the carte grise (registration document), not Crit'Air.

vocabulary.definition: A colored windshield sticker classifying a vehicle's emission level from 0 (cleanest) to 5
A designated urban area where only vehicles meeting certain emission standards may drive

questionFr: Quels documents devez-vous obligatoirement avoir à bord de votre véhicule ?
questionEn: Which documents must you compulsorily carry in your vehicle?

A.fr: Permis de conduire, carte grise et attestation d'assurance
A.en: Driving license, registration certificate, and insurance certificate

B.fr: Permis de conduire uniquement

explanationFr: Tout conducteur doit avoir à bord : le permis de conduire (valide), la carte grise (certificat d'immatriculation) du véhicule, et l'attestation d'assurance en cours de validité. L'absence de l'un de ces documents peut entraîner une amende.
explanationEn: Every driver must carry: a valid driving license, the vehicle registration certificate (carte grise), and a valid insurance certificate. Missing any of these documents can result in a fine.
trapNote: The passport is not required for driving within France. The three mandatory documents are: permis, carte grise, and assurance.

distractorNotes.B: The driving license alone is insufficient — you also need the carte grise and insurance proof.
distractorNotes.C: The Crit'Air sticker is only mandatory in ZFE-m zones, not universally required in the vehicle.
distractorNotes.D: A passport is not a required driving document for domestic travel.

vocabulary.definition: The official document identifying a vehicle and its owner, formally called certificat d'immatriculation
Proof that the vehicle is insured, typically displayed on the windshield

questionFr: Un jeune conducteur en période probatoire a un taux d'alcoolémie maximal autorisé de :
questionEn: A new driver during the probationary period has a maximum permitted blood alcohol level of:

A.fr: 0,5 g/L de sang

B.fr: 0,2 g/L de sang

explanationFr: Les conducteurs en période probatoire (3 ans, ou 2 ans avec conduite accompagnée) ont un taux d'alcoolémie maximal de 0,2 g/L de sang, soit quasiment zéro verre d'alcool. Ce seuil bas tient compte de la marge technique des éthylotests. Le taux pour les conducteurs expérimentés est de 0,5 g/L.
explanationEn: Probationary drivers (3 years, or 2 years with accompanied driving) have a maximum blood alcohol level of 0.2 g/L, meaning virtually zero glasses of alcohol. This low threshold accounts for the technical margin of breathalyzers. The limit for experienced drivers is 0.5 g/L.
trapNote: 0.2 g/L is NOT truly zero — it accounts for breathalyzer margins and trace amounts. But in practice, it means no drinking at all before driving.

distractorNotes.A: 0.5 g/L is the limit for experienced drivers, not probationary ones.
distractorNotes.C: The limit is 0.2 g/L, not absolute zero — the small margin accounts for measurement tolerance.
distractorNotes.D: 0.8 g/L is the threshold for criminal prosecution (délit), not a permitted limit.

vocabulary.definition: The initial period after obtaining a license (3 years, or 2 with AAC) with special restrictions
The concentration of alcohol in the blood, measured in grams per liter

questionFr: Quelle technique d'éco-conduite permet de réduire la consommation de carburant ?
questionEn: Which eco-driving technique helps reduce fuel consumption?

A.fr: Accélérer fortement puis freiner brusquement

B.fr: Anticiper le trafic et maintenir une vitesse régulière
B.en: Anticipate traffic and maintain a steady speed

D.fr: Garder les fenêtres ouvertes sur autoroute
D.en: Keep windows open on the motorway

explanationFr: L'anticipation et le maintien d'une vitesse régulière sont les piliers de l'éco-conduite. Cela évite les accélérations et freinages inutiles qui gaspillent du carburant. D'autres techniques incluent : passer les rapports tôt, vérifier la pression des pneus et couper le moteur à l'arrêt prolongé.
explanationEn: Anticipation and maintaining a steady speed are the pillars of eco-driving. This avoids unnecessary acceleration and braking that waste fuel. Other techniques include: shifting up early, checking tire pressure, and turning off the engine during prolonged stops.
trapNote: Driving in constant under-rev (option C) might seem fuel-efficient but actually strains the engine and can increase consumption. The engine has an optimal RPM range.

distractorNotes.A: Aggressive acceleration and braking is the opposite of eco-driving and significantly increases consumption.
distractorNotes.C: Constant under-revving puts strain on the engine and can actually increase fuel consumption.
distractorNotes.D: Open windows on the motorway increase aerodynamic drag, raising fuel consumption significantly.

vocabulary.definition: Driving techniques aimed at reducing fuel consumption, emissions, and wear
Reading the road and traffic ahead to adjust driving smoothly without sudden actions

questionFr: Pour conduire à l'étranger hors Union européenne, un conducteur français doit généralement disposer de :
questionEn: To drive abroad outside the European Union, a French driver generally needs:

A.fr: Son permis français suffit partout dans le monde
A.en: Their French license is sufficient worldwide

B.fr: Un permis de conduire international en complément de son permis français
B.en: An international driving permit in addition to their French license

C.fr: Un nouveau permis délivré par le pays de destination
C.en: A new license issued by the destination country

D.fr: Une traduction certifiée de son permis uniquement
D.en: Only a certified translation of their license

explanationFr: Hors Union européenne, un permis de conduire international (PCI) est généralement requis en complément du permis français. Le PCI est une traduction officielle du permis dans plusieurs langues. Il est délivré gratuitement par la préfecture et est valable 3 ans.
explanationEn: Outside the European Union, an International Driving Permit (IDP) is generally required in addition to the French license. The IDP is an official translation of the license into multiple languages. It is issued free by the prefecture and is valid for 3 years.
trapNote: The international permit does NOT replace the French license — you must carry BOTH. Also, within the EU, the French license alone is sufficient.

distractorNotes.A: The French license alone is not universally accepted outside the EU.
distractorNotes.C: You do not need to obtain a new license from the destination country for temporary visits.
distractorNotes.D: A simple translation is not equivalent to the official International Driving Permit.

vocabulary.definition: An official multi-language translation of a national driving license, required for driving in many non-EU countries
The local government office in France that handles administrative tasks including license issuance

questionFr: Concernant les véhicules électriques en circulation urbaine, quelle particularité représente un danger pour les piétons ?
questionEn: Regarding electric vehicles in urban traffic, what characteristic poses a danger to pedestrians?

A.fr: Leur vitesse excessive

B.fr: Leur quasi-silence à basse vitesse

explanationFr: Les véhicules électriques sont très silencieux à basse vitesse, ce qui représente un danger pour les piétons et les cyclistes qui ne les entendent pas approcher. C'est pourquoi la réglementation européenne impose un système d'alerte acoustique (AVAS) pour les véhicules électriques roulant à moins de 20 km/h.
explanationEn: Electric vehicles are very quiet at low speeds, posing a danger to pedestrians and cyclists who cannot hear them approaching. This is why European regulations require an Acoustic Vehicle Alerting System (AVAS) for electric vehicles traveling under 20 km/h.
trapNote: Electric vehicles are actually heavier than equivalent combustion vehicles (due to batteries), not lighter. Their danger is silence, not weight or speed.

distractorNotes.A: Electric vehicles do not inherently travel at excessive speeds in urban areas.
distractorNotes.C: Electric vehicles produce zero direct emissions — this is one of their advantages.
distractorNotes.D: Electric vehicles are typically heavier than equivalent combustion vehicles due to battery weight.

vocabulary.definition: An artificial sound system required on electric vehicles to alert pedestrians at low speeds
A vehicle powered by one or more electric motors using energy stored in batteries

questionFr: Sur le lieu d'un accident, après avoir protégé la zone, quelles informations devez-vous communiquer aux secours ?
questionEn: At an accident scene, after securing the area, what information must you communicate to emergency services?

A.fr: Uniquement votre nom et le lieu de l'accident
A.en: Only your name and the accident location

B.fr: Le lieu exact, la nature de l'accident, le nombre de victimes et leur état apparent
B.en: The exact location, nature of the accident, number of victims and their apparent condition

C.fr: Le numéro d'immatriculation des véhicules impliqués
C.en: The registration numbers of involved vehicles

D.fr: Les circonstances détaillées de l'accident pour le constat
D.en: Detailed accident circumstances for the report

explanationFr: Lors de l'appel aux secours (112, 15, 18), vous devez communiquer : le lieu exact de l'accident (route, commune, point kilométrique), la nature de l'accident (collision, renversement), le nombre de victimes, leur état apparent (conscient, inconscient, saigne), et les risques particuliers (incendie, matières dangereuses). Ne raccrochez jamais en premier.
explanationEn: When calling emergency services (112, 15, 18), you must communicate: the exact location (road, town, kilometric point), the nature of the accident (collision, rollover), the number of victims, their apparent condition (conscious, unconscious, bleeding), and any special risks (fire, hazardous materials). Never hang up first.
trapNote: The detailed circumstances for the insurance report (constat) come much later. During the emergency call, focus on location, nature, victims, and condition.

distractorNotes.A: This is far too little information — emergency services need details about victims and the accident nature to dispatch appropriate resources.
distractorNotes.C: Registration numbers are for the insurance report, not the emergency call.
distractorNotes.D: Detailed circumstances are for the constat amiable (insurance report), not the emergency call. Secours need medical and situational information.

vocabulary.definition: The standardized form filled out by drivers involved in an accident for insurance purposes
The collective term for fire, medical, and police emergency responders
Dangerous substances transported by vehicle that pose additional risks at an accident scene

questionFr: Quelle est la vitesse maximale autorisée pour un véhicule agricole sur route ?
questionEn: What is the maximum speed allowed for an agricultural vehicle on the road?

A.fr: 25 km/h

B.fr: 40 km/h

explanationFr: Les véhicules agricoles sont limités à 40 km/h sur route, sauf si leur carte grise indique une vitesse inférieure.
explanationEn: Agricultural vehicles are limited to 40 km/h on the road, unless their registration document indicates a lower speed.
trapNote: Many confuse 25 km/h (old regulation or specific machinery) with the current 40 km/h limit for most agricultural vehicles.

distractorNotes.A: 25 km/h was an older limit and still applies to some specific machinery, making it a plausible wrong answer
distractorNotes.C: 50 km/h is the urban speed limit, easy to confuse with agricultural vehicle limits
distractorNotes.D: 45 km/h is the limit for mopeds (cyclomoteurs), not agricultural vehicles

vocabulary.definition: Vehicle designed for farming use, subject to specific speed limits on public roads
Official document containing technical details and legal limits of a vehicle

questionFr: Dans un espace partagé (zone de rencontre), quelle est la vitesse maximale autorisée ?
questionEn: In a shared space (meeting zone), what is the maximum speed allowed?

A.fr: 30 km/h

B.fr: 20 km/h

explanationFr: La zone de rencontre impose une vitesse maximale de 20 km/h. Les piétons y ont la priorité et peuvent circuler sur la chaussée.
explanationEn: A meeting zone imposes a maximum speed of 20 km/h. Pedestrians have priority and may walk on the roadway.
trapNote: Candidates often confuse zone de rencontre (20 km/h) with zone 30 (30 km/h). The key difference is pedestrian priority on the roadway.

distractorNotes.A: 30 km/h is the limit for a zone 30, not a zone de rencontre
distractorNotes.C: 10 km/h has no official designation in French traffic zones
distractorNotes.D: 50 km/h is the standard urban limit, far too fast for a shared space

vocabulary.definition: Urban area limited to 20 km/h where pedestrians have absolute priority
The part of the road intended for vehicle traffic
Person traveling on foot

questionFr: Lors de la traversée d'une zone de travaux avec limitation temporaire à 50 km/h sur autoroute, quand pouvez-vous reprendre la vitesse normale ?
questionEn: When passing through a roadworks zone with a temporary 50 km/h limit on a motorway, when can you resume normal speed?

A.fr: Dès que les travaux ne sont plus visibles
A.en: As soon as the works are no longer visible

B.fr: Après le panneau de fin de limitation

C.en: After 200 metres with no visible works

D.fr: Dès que la voie redevient à deux files
D.en: As soon as the lane returns to two lanes

explanationFr: Une limitation temporaire de vitesse reste en vigueur jusqu'au panneau de fin de limitation ou de rappel d'une autre vitesse, jamais sur appréciation visuelle.
explanationEn: A temporary speed limit remains in force until the end-of-restriction sign or a sign indicating a different speed, never based on visual judgment.
trapNote: Many drivers resume speed once works are no longer visible, but the restriction is legally binding until the official sign cancels it.

distractorNotes.A: Visual assessment is irrelevant â€” the legal limit persists until officially cancelled by signage
distractorNotes.C: No such 200-metre rule exists in the Code de la Route
distractorNotes.D: Lane configuration changes do not automatically cancel speed restrictions

vocabulary.definition: A reduced speed limit imposed for a specific zone, often near roadworks
Sign indicating the previously imposed speed limit no longer applies

questionFr: En cas de brouillard dense, quels feux devez-vous utiliser en complément des feux de croisement ?
questionEn: In dense fog, which lights should you use in addition to dipped headlights?

A.fr: Les feux de route

B.fr: Les feux de brouillard avant uniquement

C.fr: Les feux de brouillard avant et arrière

explanationFr: En cas de brouillard dense (visibilité inférieure à 50 m), vous devez utiliser les feux de croisement accompagnés des feux de brouillard avant et arrière. La vitesse est alors limitée à 50 km/h.
explanationEn: In dense fog (visibility under 50 m), you must use dipped headlights with both front and rear fog lights. Speed is then limited to 50 km/h.
trapNote: Candidates often forget the rear fog light or think front fog lights alone are sufficient. Both front AND rear are required in dense fog.

distractorNotes.A: Full beams reflect off fog particles and reduce visibility further â€” never use them in fog
distractorNotes.B: Front fog lights alone are insufficient â€” the rear fog light is mandatory to be seen from behind
distractorNotes.D: Hazard lights are for stationary vehicles or specific warnings, not for driving in fog

vocabulary.definition: Specialized lights designed to improve visibility in fog, rain, or snow
Standard headlights that illuminate without blinding oncoming traffic
Conditions where seeing distance is significantly decreased

questionFr: Quelles règles s'appliquent au stationnement en zone bleue ? (2 réponses)
questionEn: Which rules apply to parking in a blue zone? (2 answers)

A.fr: Le disque de stationnement est obligatoire

B.fr: Le stationnement est limité à 1h30 en général

C.fr: Le stationnement est payant avec horodateur
C.en: Parking is paid with a parking meter

D.fr: Le stationnement est illimité le dimanche uniquement
D.en: Parking is unlimited on Sundays only

explanationFr: En zone bleue, le disque de stationnement est obligatoire et la durée est généralement limitée à 1h30. Ce n'est pas un stationnement payant â€” c'est un système de rotation gratuit contrôlé par le disque.
explanationEn: In a blue zone, a parking disc is mandatory and duration is generally limited to 1h30. It is not paid parking â€” it is a free rotation system controlled by the disc.
trapNote: Many confuse blue zones (free with disc) with paid parking zones. Others think the time limit varies widely, but 1h30 is the standard default.

distractorNotes.C: Blue zones are specifically free â€” parking meters indicate paid zones, which are different
distractorNotes.D: While some municipalities may relax rules on Sundays, this is not a universal blue zone rule

vocabulary.definition: Free parking area with time-limited stays enforced by a parking disc
A cardboard or plastic disc placed on the dashboard showing arrival time
A machine that collects payment for parking in paid zones

questionFr: Quelle est la règle concernant la recharge d'un véhicule électrique sur une borne publique ?
questionEn: What is the rule regarding charging an electric vehicle at a public charging station?

A.fr: Vous pouvez rester stationné après la charge complète sans limite
A.en: You can remain parked after full charge without time limit

B.fr: Vous devez libérer la place dès que la charge est terminée
B.en: You must free the space as soon as charging is complete

C.fr: Seuls les véhicules 100% électriques peuvent utiliser les bornes
C.en: Only 100% electric vehicles can use charging stations

D.fr: La charge est interdite la nuit sur voie publique
D.en: Charging is prohibited at night on public roads

explanationFr: Un véhicule électrique ou hybride rechargeable doit libérer la place de stationnement dédiée à la recharge dès que la charge est terminée. Rester stationné sans charger est considéré comme une occupation abusive.
explanationEn: An electric or plug-in hybrid vehicle must free the dedicated charging space as soon as charging is complete. Remaining parked without charging is considered improper use.
trapNote: Many drivers treat charging spots as regular parking after charging is done. The space is reserved for active charging, not general parking.

distractorNotes.A: Remaining parked after charge is complete blocks the station for others and may result in a fine
distractorNotes.C: Plug-in hybrid vehicles (hybrides rechargeables) are also entitled to use public charging stations
distractorNotes.D: No general prohibition exists against nighttime charging on public roads

vocabulary.definition: Public or private installation for recharging electric vehicle batteries
Vehicle combining an internal combustion engine with a rechargeable electric motor

questionFr: Lors d'un arrêt d'urgence sur autoroute, où devez-vous vous placer après avoir immobilisé votre véhicule sur la bande d'arrêt d'urgence ?
questionEn: During an emergency stop on a motorway, where should you position yourself after stopping your vehicle on the hard shoulder?

A.fr: Rester dans le véhicule avec la ceinture attachée
A.en: Stay in the vehicle with seatbelt fastened

B.fr: Se placer derrière la glissière de sécurité, le plus loin possible du véhicule
B.en: Position yourself behind the safety barrier, as far as possible from the vehicle

C.fr: Se tenir devant le véhicule pour signaler sa présence
C.en: Stand in front of the vehicle to signal your presence

D.fr: Rester à côté du véhicule sur la bande d'arrêt d'urgence
D.en: Stay next to the vehicle on the hard shoulder

explanationFr: En cas d'arrêt d'urgence sur autoroute, tous les occupants doivent quitter le véhicule par le côté droit et se placer derrière la glissière de sécurité, en s'éloignant du véhicule pour éviter tout risque de collision.
explanationEn: In case of an emergency stop on a motorway, all occupants must exit the vehicle from the right side and position themselves behind the safety barrier, moving away from the vehicle to avoid collision risks.
trapNote: The instinct is to stay in the car or stand nearby, but the hard shoulder is extremely dangerous â€” vehicles are hit from behind regularly.

distractorNotes.A: Staying in the vehicle on the hard shoulder is very dangerous due to risk of rear-end collision from inattentive drivers
distractorNotes.C: Standing in front of the vehicle keeps you on the road side and offers no protection from traffic
distractorNotes.D: The hard shoulder itself is a dangerous zone â€” you must get behind the barrier

vocabulary.definition: The strip along the right side of a motorway reserved for emergency stops
Metal barrier separating the road from the verge, designed to protect in case of collision

questionFr: Quelles sont les obligations lors du croisement d'un tramway à un arrêt ? (2 réponses)
questionEn: What are the obligations when passing a tram at a stop? (2 answers)

A.fr: S'arrêter si des piétons traversent pour rejoindre le trottoir
A.en: Stop if pedestrians are crossing to reach the pavement

B.fr: Ralentir et être prêt à s'arrêter
B.en: Slow down and be ready to stop

C.fr: Accélérer pour dépasser rapidement le tramway
C.en: Accelerate to pass the tram quickly

explanationFr: À l'approche d'un arrêt de tramway, vous devez ralentir et être prêt à vous arrêter. Si des piétons traversent pour rejoindre le trottoir, l'arrêt est obligatoire. Le tramway bénéficie d'une priorité quasi absolue.
explanationEn: When approaching a tram stop, you must slow down and be ready to stop. If pedestrians are crossing to reach the pavement, stopping is mandatory. Trams have near-absolute priority.
trapNote: Candidates often think they only need to slow down OR stop, but both obligations apply: always slow down, and stop if pedestrians are crossing.

distractorNotes.C: Accelerating near a tram stop is extremely dangerous given the high likelihood of pedestrian crossings
distractorNotes.D: Honking near a tram stop would cause confusion and is not an appropriate response â€” stopping is required

vocabulary.definition: Urban rail vehicle running on tracks embedded in the road surface
Designated area where trams stop to allow passengers to board or alight
Raised path alongside a road reserved for pedestrians

questionFr: Dans un convoi de véhicules, quelle distance minimale doit séparer chaque véhicule du convoi ?
questionEn: In a vehicle convoy, what minimum distance must separate each vehicle in the convoy?

A.fr: 50 mètres

B.fr: 100 mètres

explanationFr: Les véhicules d'un convoi doivent maintenir une distance d'au moins 50 mètres entre eux pour permettre aux autres usagers de dépasser en sécurité un véhicule à la fois.
explanationEn: Vehicles in a convoy must maintain a distance of at least 50 metres between them to allow other road users to safely overtake one vehicle at a time.
trapNote: Candidates often confuse this with the 2-second rule or poids lourd (heavy vehicle) 50m rule on motorways. Here it specifically concerns organized convoys.

distractorNotes.B: 100 metres is not the required convoy spacing â€” this is sometimes confused with heavy vehicle motorway following distances
distractorNotes.C: 150 metres has no specific application in convoy rules
distractorNotes.D: 200 metres is excessive and not required by the Code de la Route for convoys

vocabulary.definition: A group of vehicles traveling together in formation on public roads
Minimum space required between vehicles for safe stopping

questionFr: Quelle est la règle d'éco-conduite concernant le régime moteur optimal ?
questionEn: What is the eco-driving rule regarding optimal engine speed?

A.fr: Monter dans les tours avant de changer de vitesse pour un meilleur rendement
A.en: Rev up before changing gear for better efficiency

B.fr: Passer le rapport supérieur avant 2 500 tr/min en essence et 2 000 tr/min en diesel
B.en: Shift up before 2,500 rpm for petrol and 2,000 rpm for diesel

C.fr: Rester en 3ème vitesse le plus longtemps possible
C.en: Stay in 3rd gear as long as possible

D.fr: Utiliser le point mort en descente pour économiser du carburant
D.en: Use neutral gear downhill to save fuel

explanationFr: L'éco-conduite recommande de passer le rapport supérieur avant 2 500 tr/min pour un moteur essence et avant 2 000 tr/min pour un moteur diesel afin de réduire la consommation de carburant.
explanationEn: Eco-driving recommends shifting up before 2,500 rpm for petrol engines and before 2,000 rpm for diesel engines to reduce fuel consumption.
trapNote: Many think higher revs give better power output (true) but confuse power with fuel efficiency. Also, neutral gear downhill is dangerous and does NOT save fuel on modern cars.

distractorNotes.A: High revs waste fuel â€” eco-driving is about early upshifts at low rpm
distractorNotes.C: Staying in one gear regardless of speed is inefficient and does not follow eco-driving principles
distractorNotes.D: Neutral gear downhill removes engine braking (dangerous) and modern fuel injection already cuts fuel at zero throttle in gear

vocabulary.definition: Driving techniques aimed at reducing fuel consumption and emissions
The number of revolutions per minute of the engine crankshaft
The next gear up in the transmission sequence

questionFr: Un conducteur dont la vue nécessite une correction est-il obligé de porter ses lunettes ou lentilles en conduisant ?
questionEn: Is a driver whose vision requires correction obligated to wear glasses or contact lenses while driving?

A.fr: Oui, c'est obligatoire et mentionné sur le permis
A.en: Yes, it is mandatory and noted on the licence

B.fr: Non, c'est seulement recommandé

D.fr: Non, sauf pour les conducteurs professionnels

explanationFr: Si le permis porte la mention restrictive (code 01), le port de lunettes ou de lentilles correctrices est obligatoire lors de la conduite. Le non-respect est une infraction.
explanationEn: If the licence carries the restrictive code (code 01), wearing corrective glasses or contact lenses is mandatory while driving. Non-compliance is an offence.
trapNote: Some think the glasses restriction is advisory. It is a legal obligation coded on the licence, and driving without them is an offence equivalent to driving without a valid licence.

distractorNotes.B: The correction obligation is not a recommendation â€” it is a legal requirement enforced by licence codes
distractorNotes.C: The obligation applies on ALL roads, not just motorways
distractorNotes.D: The obligation applies to ALL drivers with the code 01 restriction, not just professionals

vocabulary.definition: A coded annotation on a driving licence indicating mandatory conditions for driving
Contact lenses prescribed to correct vision deficiencies

questionFr: Quel est le taux d'alcool maximal autorisé dans le sang pour un conducteur en période probatoire ?
questionEn: What is the maximum blood alcohol level allowed for a driver in the probationary period?

A.fr: 0,5 g/l de sang

B.fr: 0,2 g/l de sang

explanationFr: Les conducteurs en période probatoire (permis de moins de 3 ans, ou 2 ans en conduite accompagnée) sont soumis à un taux maximal de 0,2 g/l de sang, soit pratiquement zéro verre d'alcool.
explanationEn: Drivers in the probationary period (licence held for less than 3 years, or 2 years with supervised driving) are subject to a maximum of 0.2 g/l of blood, meaning practically zero glasses of alcohol.
trapNote: 0.2 g/l is NOT zero tolerance â€” it allows for trace amounts from medication or food. But practically, it means no alcohol at all.

distractorNotes.A: 0.5 g/l applies to experienced drivers, not probationary ones
distractorNotes.C: The limit is 0.2 g/l not 0.0 â€” a small margin exists for non-alcoholic sources
distractorNotes.D: 0.8 g/l is the old criminal threshold and has no relevance to probationary drivers

vocabulary.definition: The first 3 years (or 2 with conduite accompagnée) after obtaining a driving licence
The concentration of alcohol in the bloodstream, measured in grams per litre

questionFr: Lors d'un dépistage de stupéfiants par les forces de l'ordre, que se passe-t-il si le test salivaire est positif ?
questionEn: During a drug screening by law enforcement, what happens if the saliva test is positive?

A.fr: Le permis est immédiatement annulé définitivement
A.en: The licence is immediately cancelled permanently

B.fr: Une prise de sang est proposée pour confirmation
B.en: A blood test is offered for confirmation

C.fr: Le conducteur reçoit un simple avertissement
C.en: The driver receives a simple warning

explanationFr: En cas de test salivaire positif aux stupéfiants, le conducteur peut demander une vérification par prise de sang. Le permis peut être retenu immédiatement, mais l'annulation définitive requiert une décision judiciaire.
explanationEn: If the saliva test is positive for drugs, the driver can request verification by blood test. The licence may be retained immediately, but permanent cancellation requires a judicial decision.
trapNote: Candidates confuse licence retention (immediate administrative measure) with cancellation (judicial decision). The blood test is a right, not automatic.

distractorNotes.A: Permanent cancellation requires a court decision â€” the immediate measure is retention, not cancellation
distractorNotes.C: Drug driving is a serious offence â€” there is no simple warning for a positive test
distractorNotes.D: Vehicle confiscation is a possible judicial penalty but not an immediate automatic measure at the roadside

vocabulary.definition: A test conducted by law enforcement to detect the presence of illegal drugs
A quick screening method using saliva to detect recent drug use
A laboratory analysis of blood used to confirm drug or alcohol presence

questionFr: Qu'est-ce qu'un éthylotest anti-démarrage (EAD) et quand peut-il être imposé ?
questionEn: What is an alcohol interlock device (EAD) and when can it be imposed?

A.fr: Un dispositif obligatoire sur tous les véhicules neufs depuis 2024
A.en: A device mandatory on all new vehicles since 2024

B.fr: Un dispositif imposé par le juge en alternative à la suspension du permis pour conduite en état d'ivresse
B.en: A device imposed by the judge as an alternative to licence suspension for drunk driving

C.fr: Un contrôle technique supplémentaire pour les véhicules de plus de 10 ans
C.en: An additional technical inspection for vehicles over 10 years old

D.fr: Un éthylotest chimique que chaque conducteur doit avoir dans son véhicule
D.en: A chemical breathalyser that every driver must have in their vehicle

explanationFr: L'EAD est un dispositif installé dans le véhicule qui empêche le démarrage si le taux d'alcool du conducteur dépasse le seuil autorisé. Il peut être imposé par un juge comme alternative à la suspension ou annulation du permis en cas de conduite en état d'ivresse.
explanationEn: The EAD is a device installed in the vehicle that prevents starting if the driver's alcohol level exceeds the permitted threshold. It can be imposed by a judge as an alternative to licence suspension or cancellation for drunk driving.
trapNote: Many confuse the EAD (judicial device) with the chemical breathalyser (éthylotest) that was once required in all vehicles. They are completely different things.

distractorNotes.A: The EAD is not mandatory on all vehicles â€” it is a judicial measure imposed on specific offenders
distractorNotes.C: The EAD has nothing to do with technical inspections (contrôle technique)
distractorNotes.D: The in-vehicle breathalyser requirement was removed in 2020 â€” and it was never the same as an EAD

vocabulary.definition: A device that requires the driver to blow into a breathalyser before the engine will start
Temporary withdrawal of the right to drive as an administrative or judicial penalty

questionFr: Combien de points un conducteur récupère-t-il après un stage de sensibilisation à la sécurité routière ?
questionEn: How many points does a driver recover after a road safety awareness course?

A.fr: 2 points

B.fr: 4 points

explanationFr: Un stage de sensibilisation à la sécurité routière permet de récupérer 4 points, dans la limite du plafond de 12 points (ou 6 points en période probatoire). Un seul stage par an est autorisé.
explanationEn: A road safety awareness course allows recovery of 4 points, up to the maximum of 12 points (or 6 in the probationary period). Only one course per year is allowed.
trapNote: Candidates often think they recover all lost points or only 2. The fixed recovery is 4 points regardless of how many were lost.

distractorNotes.A: 2 points is the amount recovered after 6 months without infraction for a minor 1-point offence, not from a stage
distractorNotes.C: 6 points is the initial probationary total, not the number recovered per stage
distractorNotes.D: The stage only recovers 4 points, not all â€” full recovery only happens after extended infraction-free periods

vocabulary.definition: A 2-day course on road safety that allows drivers to recover up to 4 licence points
The current number of points on a driving licence out of the 12-point maximum

questionFr: Quelle est la différence principale entre la conduite accompagnée (AAC) et la conduite supervisée ?
questionEn: What is the main difference between accompanied driving (AAC) and supervised driving?

A.fr: L'AAC est accessible dès 15 ans, la conduite supervisée à partir de 18 ans
A.en: AAC is accessible from age 15, supervised driving from age 18

B.fr: La conduite supervisée n'exige aucune formation en auto-école
B.en: Supervised driving requires no driving school training

C.fr: L'AAC dure 3 ans minimum, la conduite supervisée n'a pas de durée minimale
C.en: AAC lasts at least 3 years, supervised driving has no minimum duration

D.fr: La conduite supervisée permet de conduire seul après 6 mois
D.en: Supervised driving allows driving alone after 6 months

explanationFr: L'apprentissage anticipé de la conduite (AAC) est accessible dès 15 ans avec un minimum de 3 000 km sur au moins 1 an. La conduite supervisée est accessible à partir de 18 ans, après un échec à l'examen ou en complément de formation, avec au moins 1 000 km sans durée minimale imposée.
explanationEn: Early driving training (AAC) is accessible from age 15 with a minimum of 3,000 km over at least 1 year. Supervised driving is accessible from age 18, after failing the test or as additional training, with at least 1,000 km and no minimum duration.
trapNote: Many confuse the two programmes. AAC is longer and starts younger; supervised driving is a shorter option for adults, often after a failed test.

distractorNotes.B: Both AAC and supervised driving require initial training at a driving school (20 hours minimum)
distractorNotes.C: AAC requires 1 year minimum (not 3), and supervised driving does have a distance requirement (1,000 km) but no time minimum
distractorNotes.D: Neither programme allows solo driving â€” a supervising driver must always be present until the licence is obtained

vocabulary.definition: Early driving programme from age 15 requiring 3,000 km over at least 1 year with a supervisor
Post-training driving programme from age 18 requiring 1,000 km with a supervisor
An experienced driver who supervises a learner during accompanied or supervised driving

questionFr: Quelles sont les obligations d'un conducteur concernant l'assurance automobile ? (2 réponses)
questionEn: What are a driver's obligations regarding car insurance? (2 answers)

A.fr: L'assurance responsabilité civile (au tiers) est le minimum obligatoire
A.en: Third-party liability insurance is the minimum requirement

B.fr: L'attestation d'assurance doit pouvoir être présentée lors d'un contrôle
B.en: The insurance certificate must be producible during a check

C.fr: L'assurance tous risques est obligatoire pour les véhicules de moins de 5 ans
C.en: Comprehensive insurance is mandatory for vehicles under 5 years old

D.fr: L'assurance n'est obligatoire que pour circuler sur autoroute
D.en: Insurance is only mandatory for motorway driving

explanationFr: Tout véhicule terrestre à moteur doit être couvert au minimum par une assurance responsabilité civile (au tiers). L'attestation d'assurance (carte verte ou mémo Véhicule Assuré) doit être présentable lors de tout contrôle routier.
explanationEn: Every motor land vehicle must be covered at minimum by third-party liability insurance. The insurance certificate (green card or Vehicle Insured memo) must be producible during any road check.
trapNote: Some think comprehensive (tous risques) is legally required â€” only third-party is mandatory. Also, the green card was replaced by a digital system but proof must still be available.

distractorNotes.C: No level of comprehensive insurance is legally required regardless of vehicle age â€” only third-party is mandatory
distractorNotes.D: Insurance is mandatory on ALL roads and even for vehicles that are simply parked, not just on motorways

vocabulary.definition: The minimum mandatory insurance covering damage caused to others by the insured vehicle
Document proving that a vehicle is insured, must be available for presentation to authorities
Full coverage insurance including damage to one's own vehicle â€” optional, not legally required

questionFr: Un conducteur étranger résidant en France depuis plus d'un an doit-il échanger son permis de conduire ?
questionEn: Must a foreign driver residing in France for more than one year exchange their driving licence?

A.fr: Non, un permis étranger est valable indéfiniment en France
A.en: No, a foreign licence is valid indefinitely in France

B.fr: Oui, l'échange est obligatoire dans l'année suivant l'installation en France pour les permis non-européens
B.en: Yes, exchange is mandatory within the year following establishment in France for non-European licences

C.fr: Oui, mais uniquement pour les conducteurs professionnels
C.en: Yes, but only for professional drivers

D.en: No, an international licence is sufficient

explanationFr: Les titulaires d'un permis non-européen doivent l'échanger contre un permis français dans l'année suivant l'établissement de leur résidence en France. Les permis européens (UE/EEE) sont reconnus sans échange.
explanationEn: Holders of a non-European licence must exchange it for a French licence within one year of establishing residence in France. European licences (EU/EEA) are recognised without exchange.
trapNote: EU/EEA licence holders do not need to exchange, but non-European licence holders do. Many forget the one-year deadline.

distractorNotes.A: Non-European licences become invalid for driving in France after one year of residency
distractorNotes.C: The exchange obligation applies to all drivers, not just professionals
distractorNotes.D: An international driving permit is only valid for temporary stays, not for residents

vocabulary.definition: The administrative process of converting a foreign driving licence to a French one
A translation document valid only temporarily alongside the original licence

questionFr: Qu'est-ce que la formation continue obligatoire (FCO) pour les conducteurs professionnels ?
questionEn: What is the mandatory continuous training (FCO) for professional drivers?

A.fr: Une formation de 35 heures tous les 5 ans pour conserver la qualification CPC
A.en: A 35-hour course every 5 years to maintain the CPC qualification

B.fr: Un examen médical annuel obligatoire

C.fr: Un stage de conduite de 10 heures tous les 3 ans

D.fr: Une mise à jour du permis tous les 10 ans

explanationFr: La FCO (Formation Continue Obligatoire) est une formation de 35 heures réalisée tous les 5 ans. Elle est obligatoire pour les conducteurs professionnels de transport de marchandises ou de voyageurs pour renouveler leur carte de qualification de conducteur (CPC).
explanationEn: The FCO (Mandatory Continuous Training) is a 35-hour course taken every 5 years. It is mandatory for professional drivers of goods or passenger transport to renew their Driver Certificate of Professional Competence (CPC).
trapNote: Candidates confuse FCO (professional training) with the general licence validity period or medical checks. The 35-hour/5-year cycle is very specific to CPC holders.

distractorNotes.B: Medical examinations are separate requirements and not annually mandatory for most professional drivers
distractorNotes.C: There is no 10-hour/3-year driving course requirement in the professional driver framework
distractorNotes.D: Licence renewal periods are separate from the CPC training obligation

vocabulary.definition: 35-hour refresher course required every 5 years for professional transport drivers
Mandatory qualification card for professional goods or passenger transport drivers

questionFr: En tant que conducteur, êtes-vous responsable des passagers non attachés dans votre véhicule ?
questionEn: As a driver, are you responsible for unbelted passengers in your vehicle?

A.fr: Non, chaque passager est responsable de sa propre ceinture
A.en: No, each passenger is responsible for their own seatbelt

B.fr: Oui, pour les passagers mineurs uniquement

C.fr: Oui, pour tous les passagers quel que soit leur âge
C.en: Yes, for all passengers regardless of age

explanationFr: Le conducteur est responsable du port de la ceinture pour les passagers mineurs (moins de 18 ans). Les passagers adultes sont personnellement responsables et reçoivent eux-mêmes l'amende en cas de non-port.
explanationEn: The driver is responsible for seatbelt wearing by minor passengers (under 18). Adult passengers are personally responsible and receive the fine themselves if unbelted.
trapNote: Many think the driver is responsible for ALL passengers or for NONE. The distinction is age: minors = driver's responsibility, adults = their own.

distractorNotes.A: This is only true for adult passengers â€” the driver IS responsible for minors
distractorNotes.C: The driver is only responsible for minors, not for adult passengers who are personally liable
distractorNotes.D: The driver's responsibility for minor passengers applies on all roads, not just motorways

vocabulary.definition: Safety restraint required for all vehicle occupants in France
A vehicle occupant under 18 years of age, for whom the driver bears legal responsibility

questionFr: Qu'est-ce que l'aquaplaning et à partir de quelle épaisseur d'eau le risque devient-il critique ?
questionEn: What is aquaplaning and from what depth of water does the risk become critical?

A.fr: Perte d'adhérence sur sol mouillé, risque critique à partir de 2-3 mm d'eau
A.en: Loss of grip on wet road, critical risk from 2-3 mm of water

B.fr: Blocage des freins par l'eau, risque à partir de 10 cm d'eau
B.en: Brake lock from water, risk from 10 cm of water

C.fr: Dérapage dû au verglas, risque à partir de 0Â°C
C.en: Skidding due to black ice, risk from 0Â°C

D.fr: Usure accélérée des pneus par l'eau, risque au-dessus de 50 km/h
D.en: Accelerated tyre wear from water, risk above 50 km/h

explanationFr: L'aquaplaning se produit lorsqu'une pellicule d'eau s'interpose entre les pneus et la chaussée, entraînant une perte totale d'adhérence. Le risque devient critique à partir de seulement 2-3 mm d'eau, surtout à grande vitesse ou avec des pneus usés.
explanationEn: Aquaplaning occurs when a film of water forms between the tyres and the road surface, causing total loss of grip. The risk becomes critical from just 2-3 mm of water, especially at high speed or with worn tyres.
trapNote: Most people think you need deep water for aquaplaning. In reality, just 2-3 mm is enough at highway speeds with worn tyres.

distractorNotes.B: Aquaplaning is about tyre-surface contact loss, not brake locking â€” and 10 cm is far more than needed
distractorNotes.C: This describes black ice (verglas), a completely different phenomenon from aquaplaning
distractorNotes.D: Aquaplaning does not cause tyre wear â€” it causes loss of steering and braking control

vocabulary.definition: Loss of tyre contact with the road due to a layer of water between the tyre and road surface
The ability of tyres to maintain contact and friction with the road surface
A thin layer of water on the road surface that can cause aquaplaning

questionFr: Comment peut-on détecter la présence de verglas sur la route ?
questionEn: How can you detect the presence of black ice on the road?

A.fr: La route apparaît brillante et légèrement plus foncée que d'habitude
A.en: The road appears shiny and slightly darker than usual

B.fr: La route est toujours blanche et visible comme du givre
B.en: The road is always white and visible like frost

C.fr: Le verglas est toujours annoncé par un panneau de signalisation
C.en: Black ice is always announced by a road sign

D.fr: Le verglas ne se forme qu'en dessous de -10Â°C

explanationFr: Le verglas est une fine couche de glace transparente qui rend la route brillante et légèrement plus foncée. Contrairement au givre (blanc), le verglas est presque invisible, d'où son nom anglais 'black ice'. Il se forme dès que la température au sol est proche de 0Â°C.
explanationEn: Black ice is a thin layer of transparent ice that makes the road shiny and slightly darker. Unlike frost (white), black ice is nearly invisible, hence its English name. It forms when the ground temperature is near 0Â°C.
trapNote: The biggest danger of black ice is that it's nearly invisible. Many expect ice to look white like frost, but black ice is transparent and blends with the asphalt.

distractorNotes.B: White frost is visible and easier to detect â€” black ice is transparent and much more dangerous precisely because it's hard to see
distractorNotes.C: Black ice forms unpredictably and is rarely announced by signs â€” drivers must be vigilant on their own
distractorNotes.D: Black ice can form at temperatures just below 0Â°C, not only at extreme cold

vocabulary.definition: A thin, transparent layer of ice on the road surface, nearly invisible and extremely slippery
White ice crystals visible on surfaces, formed by freezing moisture in the air
The temperature at road surface level, which can differ from air temperature

questionFr: Que signifie un panneau triangulaire indiquant une pente de 10% ?
questionEn: What does a triangular sign indicating a 10% gradient mean?

A.fr: La route monte de 10 mètres pour chaque 100 mètres parcourus
A.en: The road rises 10 metres for every 100 metres travelled

B.fr: La route est à 10 mètres d'altitude
B.en: The road is at 10 metres altitude

C.fr: La vitesse doit être réduite de 10%

explanationFr: Un gradient de 10% signifie que pour chaque 100 mètres de distance horizontale, la route monte (ou descend) de 10 mètres. Une pente forte nécessite d'adapter sa vitesse et d'utiliser le frein moteur en descente.
explanationEn: A 10% gradient means that for every 100 metres of horizontal distance, the road rises (or falls) by 10 metres. A steep gradient requires adapting speed and using engine braking on descents.
trapNote: Many interpret the percentage as a speed reduction instruction or confuse it with a distance marker. It is purely about elevation change relative to distance.

distractorNotes.B: The sign indicates gradient steepness, not altitude above sea level
distractorNotes.C: The percentage refers to the slope angle, not a speed reduction requirement
distractorNotes.D: Road narrowing uses a completely different sign â€” the gradient sign only concerns elevation change

vocabulary.definition: The steepness of a road expressed as a percentage of vertical rise over horizontal distance
Using a lower gear to slow the vehicle without relying solely on the brake pedal
A section of road that goes downhill

questionFr: Quel est le rôle des bandes sonores (bandes rugueuses) sur les bords de la chaussée ?
questionEn: What is the purpose of rumble strips on the edges of the roadway?

A.fr: Réduire le bruit de la circulation pour les riverains
A.en: Reduce traffic noise for local residents

B.fr: Alerter le conducteur qui dérive vers le bord de la route par des vibrations et du bruit
B.en: Alert the driver drifting towards the road edge through vibrations and noise

C.fr: Améliorer l'adhérence des pneus en cas de pluie
C.en: Improve tyre grip in rainy conditions

D.fr: Indiquer une zone de stationnement autorisé

explanationFr: Les bandes sonores (ou bandes rugueuses) sont des rainures ou reliefs sur le revêtement qui produisent des vibrations et un bruit caractéristique lorsqu'un véhicule les franchit. Elles alertent le conducteur somnolent ou inattentif qu'il quitte sa voie.
explanationEn: Rumble strips are grooves or raised profiles on the road surface that produce vibrations and a characteristic noise when a vehicle crosses them. They alert drowsy or inattentive drivers that they are leaving their lane.
trapNote: The name 'bandes sonores' (sound strips) might suggest they relate to noise management. Their purpose is the opposite: to CREATE noise and vibration as a safety alert.

distractorNotes.A: Rumble strips create noise to alert drivers, not to reduce noise for residents
distractorNotes.C: Rumble strips do not improve tyre grip â€” their purpose is purely alerting through vibration
distractorNotes.D: Rumble strips have no connection to parking zones

vocabulary.definition: Textured road surface features that create noise and vibration to alert drifting drivers
A state of sleepiness that impairs a driver's attention and reaction time

questionFr: Que signifient des marquages au sol temporaires de couleur jaune ?
questionEn: What do temporary yellow road markings mean?

A.fr: Ils indiquent une zone de stationnement payant
A.en: They indicate a paid parking zone

B.fr: Ils remplacent les marquages blancs permanents et doivent être suivis en priorité
B.en: They replace permanent white markings and must be followed as priority

C.fr: Ils indiquent une piste cyclable temporaire
C.en: They indicate a temporary cycle lane

explanationFr: Les marquages jaunes sont des marquages temporaires qui prévalent sur les marquages blancs permanents. Ils sont utilisés lors de travaux ou de déviations et définissent les nouvelles voies de circulation à suivre.
explanationEn: Yellow markings are temporary markings that take precedence over permanent white markings. They are used during roadworks or diversions and define the new traffic lanes to follow.
trapNote: When both yellow and white markings are visible, drivers must follow the YELLOW ones. Many instinctively follow the more familiar white lines.

distractorNotes.A: Yellow markings on the road indicate temporary traffic layout, not parking zones
distractorNotes.C: Temporary cycle lanes use different signage â€” yellow road markings concern general traffic flow changes
distractorNotes.D: Yellow temporary markings have full regulatory force and take priority over permanent white ones

vocabulary.definition: Yellow road markings used during works that override permanent white markings
Standard white road markings defining lanes, edges, and traffic flow
An alternative route set up to redirect traffic around an obstruction

questionFr: Quelle est la fonction d'une aire de refuge sur autoroute ?
questionEn: What is the purpose of an emergency refuge area on a motorway?

A.fr: Permettre aux conducteurs de se reposer et dormir
A.en: Allow drivers to rest and sleep

B.fr: Offrir un espace sûr pour s'arrêter en cas de panne ou d'urgence entre deux aires de repos
B.en: Provide a safe space to stop in case of breakdown or emergency between rest areas

C.fr: Permettre aux poids lourds de vérifier leur chargement
C.en: Allow heavy vehicles to check their load

D.fr: Servir de point de contrôle pour les péages
D.en: Serve as a checkpoint for tolls

explanationFr: Les aires de refuge sont des espaces aménagés sur les autoroutes, généralement tous les 2 km, permettant aux conducteurs de s'arrêter en sécurité en cas d'urgence. Elles disposent d'un poste d'appel d'urgence.
explanationEn: Emergency refuge areas are spaces set up on motorways, typically every 2 km, allowing drivers to stop safely in an emergency. They have an emergency telephone.
trapNote: Refuge areas are NOT rest areas â€” they are for emergencies only, not for planned stops, sleeping, or checking cargo.

distractorNotes.A: Refuge areas are strictly for emergencies â€” rest areas (aires de repos) are for planned stops and sleeping
distractorNotes.C: Load checks should be done at rest areas or dedicated facilities, not at emergency refuges
distractorNotes.D: Refuge areas have no connection to toll collection

vocabulary.definition: A safe pull-off space on motorways for emergency stops, equipped with an emergency phone
An orange phone post on motorways connected directly to emergency services

questionFr: Quels facteurs augmentent le risque d'aquaplaning ? (2 réponses)
questionEn: Which factors increase the risk of aquaplaning? (2 answers)

A.fr: Des pneus usés avec une profondeur de sculpture inférieure à 1,6 mm
A.en: Worn tyres with tread depth below 1.6 mm

B.fr: Une vitesse élevée sur route mouillée
B.en: High speed on a wet road

D.fr: Une pression des pneus trop élevée
D.en: Tyre pressure that is too high

explanationFr: L'aquaplaning est favorisé principalement par des pneus usés (qui ne peuvent plus évacuer l'eau) et une vitesse élevée (qui augmente la pression de l'eau sous le pneu). La profondeur minimale légale est de 1,6 mm mais les experts recommandent de changer les pneus dès 3 mm.
explanationEn: Aquaplaning is primarily caused by worn tyres (which can no longer disperse water) and high speed (which increases water pressure under the tyre). The legal minimum depth is 1.6 mm but experts recommend changing tyres at 3 mm.
trapNote: Over-inflated tyres can reduce contact patch but are not a primary aquaplaning factor. The key factors are speed and tread depth.

distractorNotes.C: Engine braking actually helps maintain control â€” it does not cause aquaplaning
distractorNotes.D: While over-inflation slightly reduces contact area, it is not a primary aquaplaning risk factor compared to speed and tread depth

vocabulary.definition: The depth of the grooves in a tyre that channel water away from the contact patch
A tyre whose tread has been reduced by use, decreasing its ability to grip and evacuate water
The air pressure inside a tyre, measured in bars or PSI

questionFr: Que signifie une manche à air (indicateur de vent) sur le bord de la route ?
questionEn: What does a windsock (wind indicator) on the roadside mean?

A.fr: Elle indique la proximité d'un aérodrome
A.en: It indicates the proximity of an airfield

B.fr: Elle signale une zone exposée aux vents latéraux dangereux et indique leur force
B.en: It signals an area exposed to dangerous crosswinds and indicates their strength

C.fr: Elle indique la direction de la déviation la plus proche
C.en: It indicates the direction of the nearest diversion

D.fr: Elle signale une zone où la vitesse est limitée en cas de vent
D.en: It signals a zone where speed is limited in case of wind

explanationFr: Les manches à air installées sur les routes (ponts, viaducs, zones ouvertes) signalent des zones exposées aux vents latéraux. L'horizontalité de la manche indique la force du vent : horizontale = vent très fort (danger maximal).
explanationEn: Windsocks installed on roads (bridges, viaducts, open areas) signal zones exposed to crosswinds. The angle of the sock indicates wind strength: horizontal = very strong wind (maximum danger).
trapNote: Many associate windsocks only with airports. On roads, they serve as real-time wind indicators for drivers, especially on bridges and exposed stretches.

distractorNotes.A: While windsocks are common at airfields, roadside windsocks serve a driving safety purpose for crosswind warning
distractorNotes.C: Windsocks indicate wind direction and force, not diversion routes
distractorNotes.D: No automatic speed limit is triggered by windsocks â€” they provide information for the driver to adapt

vocabulary.definition: A fabric tube mounted on a pole that indicates wind direction and strength
Wind blowing perpendicular to the direction of travel, which can destabilize vehicles
A long elevated bridge carrying a road over a valley or other obstacle

questionFr: Lors de l'approche d'un péage sur autoroute, que devez-vous faire ?
questionEn: When approaching a motorway toll booth, what should you do?

A.fr: Maintenir votre vitesse jusqu'à la barrière de péage
A.en: Maintain your speed until the toll barrier

B.fr: Ralentir progressivement et choisir la file correspondant à votre mode de paiement
B.en: Slow down gradually and choose the lane matching your payment method

C.fr: S'arrêter sur la bande d'arrêt d'urgence pour préparer votre paiement
C.en: Stop on the hard shoulder to prepare your payment

D.fr: Changer de file au dernier moment pour trouver la plus courte
D.en: Change lanes at the last moment to find the shortest one

explanationFr: À l'approche d'un péage, vous devez ralentir progressivement et vous diriger vers la file correspondant à votre mode de paiement (télépéage, carte bancaire, espèces). Les voies sont signalées par des pictogrammes au-dessus de chaque file.
explanationEn: When approaching a toll, you must slow down gradually and head for the lane matching your payment method (electronic toll, bank card, cash). Lanes are marked by pictograms above each lane.
trapNote: Last-minute lane changes near toll booths are a major accident cause. Drivers should choose their lane well in advance based on payment method signs.

distractorNotes.A: Maintaining speed towards the toll is dangerous â€” you must begin slowing well before the barrier
distractorNotes.C: The hard shoulder is for emergencies only, never for payment preparation
distractorNotes.D: Last-minute lane changes at toll approaches are extremely dangerous and cause many accidents

vocabulary.definition: A point on a motorway where drivers pay for road usage
An automatic payment system using an in-vehicle transponder to pass tolls without stopping
The physical barrier at a toll station that lifts after payment

questionFr: Quels sont les panneaux qui signalent une zone de fréquence élevée de traversée d'animaux sauvages ? (2 réponses)
questionEn: Which signs indicate a zone of high frequency of wild animal crossings? (2 answers)

A.fr: Un panneau triangulaire avec une silhouette de cerf
A.en: A triangular sign with a deer silhouette

B.fr: Un panonceau indiquant la distance de la zone concernée
B.en: A supplementary plate indicating the distance of the affected zone

C.fr: Un panneau rond bleu avec un animal
C.en: A round blue sign with an animal

explanationFr: Les zones de traversée d'animaux sauvages sont signalées par un panneau triangulaire de danger (type A15a) montrant une silhouette d'animal, souvent accompagné d'un panonceau de distance précisant l'étendue de la zone dangereuse.
explanationEn: Wild animal crossing zones are marked with a triangular danger sign (type A15a) showing an animal silhouette, often accompanied by a distance plate specifying the extent of the danger zone.
trapNote: Many forget the distance plate that accompanies the animal crossing sign. Without it, drivers don't know how long to remain vigilant.

distractorNotes.C: Round blue signs indicate obligations (mandatory direction, lanes), not animal crossing warnings
distractorNotes.D: Flashing amber lights are used for specific hazard warnings but not specifically for animal crossing zones

vocabulary.definition: A triangular sign with red border warning of a specific hazard ahead
A small rectangular sign placed below a main sign providing additional information
A zone where wild animals frequently cross the road

questionFr: Un piéton accompagné d'un chien guide d'aveugle traverse la route. Que devez-vous faire ?
questionEn: A pedestrian accompanied by a guide dog for the blind is crossing the road. What should you do?

A.fr: Klaxonner pour signaler votre présence

B.fr: S'arrêter et lui laisser la priorité absolue
B.en: Stop and give them absolute priority

C.en: Slow down and carefully go around them

D.fr: Faire des appels de phares pour l'avertir
D.en: Flash your headlights to warn them

explanationFr: Une personne aveugle ou malvoyante avec un chien guide ou une canne blanche bénéficie d'une priorité absolue. Tout conducteur doit s'arrêter et attendre que la personne ait terminé sa traversée. Le klaxon ou les appels de phares sont inutiles et peuvent désorienter le chien guide.
explanationEn: A blind or visually impaired person with a guide dog or white cane has absolute priority. All drivers must stop and wait until the person has completed their crossing. Honking or flashing lights is useless and can disorient the guide dog.
trapNote: Honking or flashing seems helpful but is counterproductive â€” it can confuse the guide dog and disorient the person. Complete stop and patience is required.

distractorNotes.A: Honking can disorient the guide dog and frighten the pedestrian â€” it is the wrong response
distractorNotes.C: Going around is dangerous â€” the person may not be aware of your vehicle and could change direction
distractorNotes.D: Flashing headlights is useless for a blind person and may confuse the guide dog

vocabulary.definition: A specially trained dog that helps visually impaired people navigate safely
A mobility aid used by visually impaired people to detect obstacles
The highest level of right of way, requiring all other road users to stop

questionFr: Comment sont classés les fauteuils roulants électriques (scooters de mobilité) dans le Code de la Route ?
questionEn: How are electric wheelchairs (mobility scooters) classified in the Highway Code?

A.fr: Comme des véhicules à moteur soumis au permis
A.en: As motor vehicles requiring a licence

B.fr: Comme des piétons lorsqu'ils circulent à allure du pas
B.en: As pedestrians when they travel at walking pace

C.fr: Comme des cyclistes devant emprunter les pistes cyclables
C.en: As cyclists who must use cycle lanes

D.fr: Comme des cyclomoteurs limités à 45 km/h

explanationFr: Les personnes en fauteuil roulant électrique ou scooter de mobilité sont assimilées aux piétons lorsqu'elles circulent à allure du pas. Elles peuvent emprunter les trottoirs et les passages piétons et bénéficient des mêmes droits et protections.
explanationEn: People in electric wheelchairs or mobility scooters are classified as pedestrians when travelling at walking pace. They may use pavements and pedestrian crossings and benefit from the same rights and protections.
trapNote: Despite being motorised, mobility scooters at walking pace are legally pedestrians â€” not vehicles, cyclists, or mopeds.

distractorNotes.A: Mobility scooters at walking pace are not classified as motor vehicles and require no licence
distractorNotes.C: They are not cyclists â€” they use pedestrian infrastructure, not cycle lanes
distractorNotes.D: Mobility scooters are limited to walking pace (6 km/h), nowhere near the 45 km/h moped classification

vocabulary.definition: A motorised chair used by people with mobility impairments
A speed equivalent to that of a pedestrian, approximately 6 km/h
A small motorised vehicle for people with reduced mobility

questionFr: Quelles règles s'appliquent aux quads (VTT à moteur) sur la voie publique ?
questionEn: What rules apply to quad bikes on public roads?

A.fr: Ils sont interdits sur toutes les voies publiques
A.en: They are prohibited on all public roads

B.fr: Ils sont autorisés s'ils sont homologués route, immatriculés et assurés
B.en: They are allowed if road-approved, registered and insured

C.fr: Ils sont autorisés uniquement sur les chemins ruraux
C.en: They are allowed only on rural paths

D.fr: Ils sont autorisés sans restriction sur toutes les routes
D.en: They are allowed without restriction on all roads

explanationFr: Les quads peuvent circuler sur la voie publique à condition d'être homologués route, immatriculés et assurés. Le conducteur doit posséder le permis approprié (B ou AM selon la cylindrée) et porter un casque homologué.
